Implement the following plan:

# Plan: ClickUp → HHP Task Management Migration

## Context
Replace ClickUp with HHP as the sole internal task management system. The current `/tasks` page is a lightweight manual system with 16 fields, no service layer, no threaded comments, no file attachments, no recurring engine, and no dashboards. This plan adds all missing features in 6 milestones, starting with M1 (Foundation).

**Key decisions:**
- Dashboards = separate pages (`/tasks/my-dashboard`, `/tasks/admin`, `/tasks/client/[id]`)
- Recurring = auto-clone on complete (immediate), plus cron as safety net
- Start with M1: Foundation (service layer + schema)

---

## Milestone 1: Foundation (START HERE)

**Goal:** Extract inline Supabase calls into a service class. Add new columns to `tasks` table. No visible UI changes.

### 1a. DB Migration: `026_tasks_schema_additions.sql`
Apply via Supabase MCP tool:
```sql
ALTER TABLE tasks
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS client_id UUID REFERENCES clients(id) ON DELETE SET NULL,
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS parent_task_id UUID REFERENCES tasks(id) ON DELETE CASCADE,
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS priority TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'medium',
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS completed_at TIMESTAMPTZ,
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS recurring_config JSONB,
  ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS template_id UUID;

CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_tasks_assigned_to ON tasks(assigned_to);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_tasks_client_id ON tasks(client_id);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_tasks_status ON tasks(status);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_tasks_due_date ON tasks(due_date);
CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_tasks_parent_task_id ON tasks(parent_task_id);
```

### 1b. New file: `lib/taskService.ts`
Static class following `ClientService`/`CRMService` pattern. Export `Task` type extended with new columns. Methods:
- `getAllTasks()`, `getTaskById()`, `createTask()`, `updateTask()`, `deleteTask()`
- `updateField(id, field, value)` — for inline edits
- `reorderTasks(moves: {id, sort_order}[])` — for reorder
- `getTasksForUser(userId)`, `getTasksForClient(clientId)`
- `getSubtasks(parentTaskId)`, `getOverdueTasks()`, `getTasksDueSoon(days)`
- On status change to `complete` → set `completed_at`; on un-complete → clear it

### 1c. Refactor: `app/tasks/page.tsx`
- Import `Task` type from `@/lib/taskService`
- Replace all `supabase.from('tasks')` calls with `TaskService.*` methods
- No UI/behavior changes — everything works identically

### Files touched:
- **New:** `lib/taskService.ts`
- **Modified:** `app/tasks/page.tsx` (refactor only)
- **Migration:** `026_tasks_schema_additions.sql`

---

## Milestone 2: Comments, Attachments, Client Linking

### 2a. Migration: `027_task_comments_and_attachments.sql`
- `task_comments` table: id, task_id, user_id, user_name, content, parent_comment_id (for threading), created_at, updated_at
- `task_attachments` table: id, task_id, file_name, file_url, file_size, mime_type, uploaded_by, uploaded_by_name, created_at
- RLS: authenticated read, owner write, admin delete

### 2b. Migration: `028_task_attachments_storage.sql`
- Storage bucket `task-attachments` (private, 10MB limit)
- Pattern from `018_create_form_attachments_storage.sql`

### 2c. Service additions to `taskService.ts`
- Comment CRUD: `getComments()`, `addComment()`, `updateComment()`, `deleteComment()`
- Attachment CRUD: `getAttachments()`, `uploadAttachment()`, `deleteAttachment()`
- Auto-update `latest_comment` on tasks table when new comment added (backward compat)

### 2d. New UI components
- `components/tasks/TaskDetailModal.tsx` — extracted from page.tsx dialog, adds client_id dropdown, priority selector, comments section, attachments section
- `components/tasks/TaskComments.tsx` — threaded comment display with reply/edit/delete
- `components/tasks/TaskAttachments.tsx` — drag-drop upload, file list with download/delete

### 2e. page.tsx changes
- Add "Client" column to table
- Replace inline dialog with `TaskDetailModal`
- Comment cell shows count badge → click opens detail modal

---

## Milestone 3: Dashboards + Subtasks/Checklists

### 3a. Migration: `029_task_checklist_items.sql`
- `task_checklist_items`: id, task_id, text, is_done, display_order, created_at

### 3b. Service additions
- Checklist CRUD: `getChecklist()`, `addChecklistItem()`, `toggleChecklistItem()`, `deleteChecklistItem()`
- Dashboard queries: `getDashboardStats(userId)`, `getAdminDashboardStats()`, `getClientDashboardData(clientId)`

### 3c. New pages (separate routes)
- `app/tasks/my-dashboard/page.tsx` — My assigned tasks, overdue, due this week, completed this week
- `app/tasks/admin/page.tsx` — Admin/super_admin only. Tasks per member, per client, overdue counts, stale tasks
- `app/tasks/client/[clientId]/page.tsx` — Per-client task view with deliverables

### 3d. New components
- `components/tasks/TaskChecklist.tsx` — inline checklist in detail modal
- `components/tasks/SubtaskList.tsx` — subtask list using parent_task_id

### 3e. Sidebar changes
- Add sub-nav under Tasks: "All Tasks", "My Dashboard", "Admin Overview" (admin only)

---

## Milestone 4: Recurring Engine + Automations

### 4a. Migration: `030_task_automations.sql`
- `task_automations`: trigger_type, trigger_config, action_type, action_config, scope
- `task_automation_logs`: execution audit trail

### 4b. Recurring logic in `taskService.ts`
- On `updateTask`/`updateField` status → `complete`: if task has `recurring_config`, auto-clone with next due date, status `to_do`
- `calculateNextDueDate()` helper for weekly/monthly/daily

### 4c. API route: `app/api/tasks/cron/route.ts`
- Daily cron safety net: generate missed recurring instances, evaluate stale rules
- `vercel.json` cron config

### 4d. UI
- Recurring config editor in task detail modal (day of week, day of month, end date)
- `app/tasks/automations/page.tsx` — admin rule management + execution log

---

## Milestone 5: TG Bot Alerts + Weekly Reports

### 5a. API routes
- `app/api/tasks/alerts/route.ts` — daily 9 AM: overdue task notifications per user via TG
- `app/api/tasks/weekly-report/route.ts` — Friday 5 PM: completed/overdue/stale summary to ops chat

### 5b. 7-day stale rule
- Stale badge on task rows (updated_at > 7 days, not complete/paused)
- TG notification to assignee

### 5c. Uses existing `lib/telegramService.ts` for sending

---

## Milestone 6: Templates + Form→Task

### 6a. Migration: `031_task_templates.sql`
- `task_templates`: reusable task presets with checklist items
- `form_task_mappings`: link forms to auto-create tasks on submission

### 6b. Service additions
- Template CRUD, `createTaskFromTemplate()`, `saveTaskAsTemplate()`
- `createTaskFromFormResponse()` with field placeholder substitution

### 6c. UI
- `app/tasks/templates/page.tsx` — manage templates
- "Save as Template" button in task detail modal
- Form builder: "Auto-create task on submission" toggle

---

## Verification (per milestone)
- M1: All existing task CRUD works identically. Build passes. New columns visible in Supabase.
- M2: Create comment → appears threaded. Upload file → appears in attachments. Client dropdown works.
- M3: My Dashboard shows assigned tasks. Admin Dashboard shows per-member stats. Client view filters correctly.
- M4: Complete a weekly recurring task → new instance auto-created with next due date.
- M5: Overdue task → TG bot message received. Friday report sent to ops chat.
- M6: Create template → create task from it with prefilled fields. Form submission → task auto-created.

Perfect! Now I have enough information. Let me create a comprehensive summary report:

## Service Layer Pattern Analysis Report

Based on my analysis of the KOL Campaign Manager project, here's the complete breakdown of the service layer architecture:

### ClientService (Representative Service File)

Full content available in `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/lib/clientService.ts` (310 lines):

**Key Patterns Identified:**

#### 1. **Supabase Import & Client Setup**
```typescript
import { supabase } from './supabase';
import { Database } from './database.types';
```
- Uses a singleton browser client from `/lib/supabase.ts`
- Browser client created with `createBrowserClient<Database>()` from `@supabase/ssr`
- Includes TypeScript-generated database types from `database.types.ts`

#### 2. **Service Architecture**
- **Static class pattern**: All methods are static (no instantiation needed)
- **Modular methods**: Each method handles a single responsibility
- **Type definitions**: Custom interfaces for enhanced type safety (e.g., `ClientWithAccess` extends `Client`)
- **No constructor or state management** - purely functional wrapper around Supabase

#### 3. **Method Structure (Consistent Pattern)**
```typescript
static async methodName(params): Promise<ReturnType> {
  try {
    const { data, error } = await supabase
      .from('table_name')
      .operation()
      .conditions();
    
    if (error) throw error;
    return data || [];
  } catch (error) {
    console.error('Error message:', error);
    throw error;
  }
}
```

#### 4. **Error Handling**
- Check for Supabase `error` object after every query
- Throw errors to caller
- Console.error for debugging
- Some services (like CRMService) use non-critical error handling for auxiliary operations

#### 5. **Data Type Extraction**
```typescript
type Client = Database['public']['Tables']['clients']['Row'];
type ClientAccessMember = Database['public']['Tables']['client_access_members']['Row'];
```
- Extracts Row types from generated database types
- Prevents manual type duplication
- Ensures types always match database schema

#### 6. **Query Patterns in ClientService:**
- **Read operations**: `.select()` with joins using backtick notation
- **Count operations**: `table(count)` for aggregation without fetching full rows
- **Filtering**: `.eq()`, `.is()`, `.in()` for conditional queries
- **Ordering**: `.order('field', { ascending: boolean })`
- **Joins**: Nested selection with foreign key references
- **Single vs Multiple**: `.single()` for single record, array return for multiple

#### 7. **Computed/Enhanced Types**
ClientService returns `ClientWithAccess` which enriches the base `Client` type:
```typescript
export interface ClientWithAccess extends Client {
  member_count?: number;
  campaign_count?: number;
  whitelist_partner_name?: string;
}
```
- Maps aggregated counts from Supabase joins to properties
- Denormalizes related data (whitelist partner name)

---

### CRMService (Comparison Example)

Located at `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/lib/crmService.ts` - demonstrates more advanced patterns:

**Additional Patterns:**

1. **Stage-based entities**: Opportunities, Partners, Affiliates all track stages
2. **Audit trail**: `recordStageHistory()` tracks all stage transitions
3. **Complex joins**: Multi-table selects with aliases:
   ```typescript
   .select(`
     *,
     affiliate:crm_affiliates(*),
     client:clients!crm_opportunities_client_id_fkey(id, name)
   `)
   ```

4. **Business logic in service**: Sets `qualified_at`, `closed_at` timestamps automatically on stage changes
5. **Batch operations**: `updateOpportunityPositions()` uses `Promise.all()`
6. **Utility methods**: `updateLastContacted()`, `getOpportunityStages()` (static option builders)
7. **Non-critical error handling**: Stage history recording doesn't throw (logged but continues)

---

### Database Type System

Generated in `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/lib/database.types.ts`:

**Type Structure (3 variants per table):**
- `Row`: Represents a database record (read-only)
- `Insert`: Defines required/optional fields for creation
- `Update`: All fields optional for partial updates
- **No Task table exists** - project manages tasks through campaigns, contents, and opportunity stages

**Tables in this project:**
- Core: `clients`, `campaigns`, `campaign_kols`, `contents`, `users`
- CRM: `crm_opportunities`, `crm_partners`, `crm_affiliates`, `crm_contacts`, `crm_contact_links`, `crm_stage_history`
- Templates: `campaign_templates`, `campaign_template_budget_allocations`
- Messaging: `message_templates`, `chat_sessions`, `chat_messages`
- Tracking: `payments`, `campaign_updates`, `campaign_report_files`
- Lists: `lists`, `list_kols`
- KOLs: `master_kols`

---

### Key Files Summary

| File | Purpose | Key Pattern |
|------|---------|-------------|
| `/lib/supabase.ts` | Browser client singleton | `createBrowserClient<Database>()` |
| `/lib/supabase-server.ts` | Server-side client factory | Async function returning cookie-aware client |
| `/lib/clientService.ts` | Client CRUD + access control | Role-based queries, multi-join selection |
| `/lib/crmService.ts` | CRM entity management | Static CRUD per entity, stage tracking, audit trail |
| `/lib/database.types.ts` | Generated TypeScript definitions | Database schema as types (32KB file) |

**No task-related interfaces exist separately** - the project uses existing domain models (campaigns, contents, opportunities) to manage work.

 `./lib/clientService.ts`

```text
     1→import { supabase } from './supabase';
     2→import { Database } from './database.types';
     3→import { generateUniqueSlug } from './slugUtils';
     4→
     5→type Client = Database['public']['Tables']['clients']['Row'];
     6→type ClientAccessMember = Database['public']['Tables']['client_access_members']['Row'];
     7→
     8→export interface ClientWithAccess extends Client {
     9→  member_count?: number;
    10→  campaign_count?: number;
    11→  whitelist_partner_name?: string;
    12→}
    13→
    14→export class ClientService {
    15→  /**
    16→   * Get clients based on user role
    17→   * - Admins: See all clients
    18→   * - Members: See only clients they have access to via client_access_members
    19→   */
    20→  static async getClientsForUser(userRole: 'super_admin' | 'admin' | 'member' | 'client', userId: string): Promise<ClientWithAccess[]> {
    21→    try {
    22→      if (userRole === 'admin' || userRole === 'super_admin') {
    23→        // Admins and super admins can see all non-archived clients
    24→        const { data: clients, error } = await supabase
    25→          .from('clients')
    26→          .select(`
    27→            *,
    28→            client_access_members(count),
    29→            campaigns!campaigns_client_id_fkey(count),
    30→            whitelist_partner:partners!clients_whitelist_partner_id_fkey(name)
    31→          `)
    32→          .is('archived_at', null)
    33→          .order('created_at', { ascending: false });
    34→
    35→        if (error) throw error;
    36→
    37→        return clients?.map(client => ({
    38→          ...client,
    39→          member_count: client.client_access_members?.[0]?.count || 0,
    40→          campaign_count: (client as any).campaigns?.[0]?.count || 0,
    41→          whitelist_partner_name: (client.whitelist_partner as any)?.name
    42→        })) || [];
    43→      } else {
    44→        // Members can only see non-archived clients they have access to
    45→        const { data: clientAccess, error } = await supabase
    46→          .from('client_access_members')
    47→          .select(`
    48→            clients!inner(
    49→              id,
    50→              name,
    51→              email,
    52→              location,
    53→              is_active,
    54→              archived_at,
    55→              created_at,
    56→              updated_at,
    57→              logo_url,
    58→              campaigns!campaigns_client_id_fkey(count),
    59→              whitelist_partner:partners!clients_whitelist_partner_id_fkey(name)
    60→            )
    61→          `)
    62→          .eq('user_id', userId);
    63→
    64→        if (error) throw error;
    65→
    66→        return clientAccess?.map(access => {
    67→          const client = (access as any).clients;
    68→          return {
    69→            ...client,
    70→            campaign_count: client.campaigns?.[0]?.count || 0,
    71→            whitelist_partner_name: (client.whitelist_partner as any)?.name
    72→          };
    73→        }).filter(c => c && !c.archived_at) || [];
    74→      }
    75→    } catch (error) {
    76→      console.error('Error fetching clients:', error);
    77→      throw error;
    78→    }
    79→  }
    80→
    81→  /**
    82→   * Get all non-archived clients (admin only)
    83→   */
    84→  static async getAllClients(): Promise<Client[]> {
    85→    try {
    86→      const { data, error } = await supabase
    87→        .from('clients')
    88→        .select('*')
    89→        .is('archived_at', null)
    90→        .order('created_at', { ascending: false });
    91→
    92→      if (error) throw error;
    93→      return data || [];
    94→    } catch (error) {
    95→      console.error('Error fetching all clients:', error);
    96→      throw error;
    97→    }
    98→  }
    99→
   100→  /**
   101→   * Create a new client
   102→   */
   103→  static async createClient(
   104→    name: string,
   105→    email: string,
   106→    location?: string,
   107→    source?: string,
   108→    onboarding_call_held?: boolean,
   109→    onboarding_call_date?: string | null,
   110→    is_whitelisted?: boolean,
   111→    whitelist_partner_id?: string | null
   112→  ): Promise<Client> {
   113→    try {
   114→      // Generate a unique slug from the client name
   115→      const slug = generateUniqueSlug(name);
   116→
   117→      const { data, error } = await supabase
   118→        .from('clients')
   119→        .insert({ name, email, location, source, onboarding_call_held, onboarding_call_date, is_whitelisted, whitelist_partner_id, slug })
   120→        .select()
   121→        .single();
   122→      if (error) throw error;
   123→      return data;
   124→    } catch (error) {
   125→      console.error('Error creating client:', error);
   126→      throw error;
   127→    }
   128→  }
   129→
   130→  /**
   131→   * Update client
   132→   */
   133→  static async updateClient(id: string, updates: Partial<Pick<Client, 'name' | 'email' | 'location' | 'is_active' | 'is_whitelisted' | 'whitelist_partner_id' | 'logo_url' | 'approved_domains'>>): Promise<Client> {
   134→    try {
   135→      const { data, error } = await supabase
   136→        .from('clients')
   137→        .update(updates)
   138→        .eq('id', id)
   139→        .select()
   140→        .single();
   141→
   142→      if (error) throw error;
   143→      return data;
   144→    } catch (error) {
   145→      console.error('Error updating client:', error);
   146→      throw error;
   147→    }
   148→  }
   149→
   150→  /**
   151→   * Grant user access to a client
   152→   */
   153→  static async grantClientAccess(clientId: string, userId: string): Promise<ClientAccessMember> {
   154→    try {
   155→      const { data, error } = await supabase
   156→        .from('client_access_members')
   157→        .insert({ client_id: clientId, user_id: userId })
   158→        .select()
   159→        .single();
   160→
   161→      if (error) throw error;
   162→      return data;
   163→    } catch (error) {
   164→      console.error('Error granting client access:', error);
   165→      throw error;
   166→    }
   167→  }
   168→
   169→  /**
   170→   * Revoke user access from a client
   171→   */
   172→  static async revokeClientAccess(clientId: string, userId: string): Promise<void> {
   173→    try {
   174→      const { error } = await supabase
   175→        .from('client_access_members')
   176→        .delete()
   177→        .eq('client_id', clientId)
   178→        .eq('user_id', userId);
   179→
   180→      if (error) throw error;
   181→    } catch (error) {
   182→      console.error('Error revoking client access:', error);
   183→      throw error;
   184→    }
   185→  }
   186→
   187→  /**
   188→   * Get users with access to a specific client
   189→   */
   190→  static async getClientMembers(clientId: string): Promise<any[]> {
   191→    try {
   192→      const { data, error } = await supabase
   193→        .from('client_access_members')
   194→        .select(`
   195→          *,
   196→          users!inner(id, name, email, role)
   197→        `)
   198→        .eq('client_id', clientId);
   199→
   200→      if (error) throw error;
   201→      return data?.map(access => access.users) || [];
   202→    } catch (error) {
   203→      console.error('Error fetching client members:', error);
   204→      throw error;
   205→    }
   206→  }
   207→
   208→  /**
   209→   * Get client by ID or slug (for portal access)
   210→   */
   211→  static async getClientByIdOrSlug(identifier: string): Promise<Client | null> {
   212→    try {
   213→      // Check if identifier is a UUID
   214→      const isUUID = /^[0-9a-f]{8}-[0-9a-f]{4}-[0-9a-f]{4}-[0-9a-f]{4}-[0-9a-f]{12}$/i.test(identifier);
   215→
   216→      const query = supabase
   217→        .from('clients')
   218→        .select('*')
   219→        .is('archived_at', null);
   220→
   221→      if (isUUID) {
   222→        query.eq('id', identifier);
   223→      } else {
   224→        query.eq('slug', identifier);
   225→      }
   226→
   227→      const { data, error } = await query.single();
   228→
   229→      if (error) {
   230→        if (error.code === 'PGRST116') return null; // Not found
   231→        throw error;
   232→      }
   233→      return data;
   234→    } catch (error) {
   235→      console.error('Error fetching client by ID or slug:', error);
   236→      throw error;
   237→    }
   238→  }
   239→
   240→  /**
   241→   * Get client with their campaigns for portal display
   242→   */
   243→  static async getClientWithCampaigns(clientId: string): Promise<{
   244→    client: Client;
   245→    campaigns: any[];
   246→  } | null> {
   247→    try {
   248→      // Get client
   249→      const { data: client, error: clientError } = await supabase
   250→        .from('clients')
   251→        .select('*')
   252→        .eq('id', clientId)
   253→        .is('archived_at', null)
   254→        .single();
   255→
   256→      if (clientError || !client) return null;
   257→
   258→      // Get campaigns for this client with KOL and content counts
   259→      const { data: campaigns, error: campaignsError } = await supabase
   260→        .from('campaigns')
   261→        .select(`
   262→          id,
   263→          name,
   264→          slug,
   265→          status,
   266→          total_budget,
   267→          start_date,
   268→          end_date,
   269→          region,
   270→          description,
   271→          share_report_publicly,
   272→          created_at,
   273→          campaign_kols(count),
   274→          contents(
   275→            impressions,
   276→            likes,
   277→            comments,
   278→            retweets,
   279→            bookmarks
   280→          )
   281→        `)
   282→        .eq('client_id', clientId)
   283→        .is('archived_at', null)
   284→        .order('start_date', { ascending: false });
   285→
   286→      if (campaignsError) throw campaignsError;
   287→
   288→      // Process campaigns to add aggregated metrics
   289→      const processedCampaigns = campaigns?.map(campaign => {
   290→        const contents = (campaign as any).contents || [];
   291→        const totalImpressions = contents.reduce((sum: number, c: any) => sum + (c.impressions || 0), 0);
   292→        const totalEngagement = contents.reduce((sum: number, c: any) =>
   293→          sum + (c.likes || 0) + (c.comments || 0) + (c.retweets || 0) + (c.bookmarks || 0), 0);
   294→
   295→        return {
   296→          ...campaign,
   297→          kol_count: (campaign as any).campaign_kols?.[0]?.count || 0,
   298→          content_count: contents.length,
   299→          total_impressions: totalImpressions,
   300→          total_engagement: totalEngagement,
   301→        };
   302→      }) || [];
   303→
   304→      return { client, campaigns: processedCampaigns };
   305→    } catch (error) {
   306→      console.error('Error fetching client with campaigns:', error);
   307→      throw error;
   308→    }
   309→  }
   310→}
```
</details></tool-use>

     1→import { supabase } from './supabase';
     2→
     3→// ============================================
     4→// TypeScript Types for CRM Entities
     5→// ============================================
     6→
     7→// Opportunity Stages
     8→export type OpportunityStage =
     9→  | 'new' | 'contacted' | 'qualified' | 'unqualified' | 'nurture' | 'dead'  // Lead stages
    10→  | 'deal_qualified' | 'proposal' | 'negotiation' | 'contract' | 'closed_won' | 'closed_lost'  // Deal stages
    11→  | 'account_active' | 'account_at_risk' | 'account_churned'  // Account stages
    12→  | 'cold_dm' | 'warm' | 'tg_intro' | 'booked' | 'discovery_done' | 'proposal_sent' | 'proposal_call' | 'v2_contract' | 'v2_closed_won' | 'orbit' | 'v2_closed_lost';  // Sales Pipeline v2 stages
    13→
    14→export type AccountType = 'general' | 'channel' | 'campaign' | 'lite' | 'ad_hoc';
    15→export type OpportunitySource = 'referral' | 'inbound' | 'event' | 'cold_outreach';
    16→
    17→export type AffiliateStatus = 'new' | 'active' | 'inactive';
    18→export type PartnerStatus = 'active' | 'inactive';
    19→export type PartnerCategory = 'service_provider' | 'investor_vc' | 'project' | 'individual';
    20→
    21→export type LinkedType = 'opportunity' | 'partner' | 'affiliate';
    22→
    23→// ============================================
    24→// Interfaces
    25→// ============================================
    26→
    27→export interface CRMAffiliate {
    28→  id: string;
    29→  name: string;
    30→  affiliation: string | null;
    31→  category: string | null;
    32→  status: AffiliateStatus;
    33→  commission_model: string | null;
    34→  commission_rate: number | null;
    35→  terms_of_interest: string | null;
    36→  last_contacted_at: string | null;
    37→  owner_id: string | null;
    38→  poc_name: string | null;
    39→  poc_email: string | null;
    40→  poc_telegram: string | null;
    41→  notes: string | null;
    42→  created_at: string;
    43→  updated_at: string;
    44→}
    45→
    46→export interface CRMOpportunity {
    47→  id: string;
    48→  name: string;
    49→  stage: OpportunityStage;
    50→  account_type: AccountType | null;
    51→  deal_value: number | null;
    52→  currency: string;
    53→  last_contacted_at: string | null;
    54→  last_message_at: string | null;       // When lead/others last messaged in TG group
    55→  last_reply_at: string | null;          // When our bot last messaged in TG group
    56→  last_team_message_at: string | null;   // When a team member last messaged in TG group
    57→  owner_id: string | null;
    58→  source: OpportunitySource | null;
    59→  referrer: string | null;
    60→  gc: string | null;               // Telegram group chat ID
    61→  affiliate_id: string | null;
    62→  client_id: string | null;        // Links account-stage opportunities to clients
    63→  scope: string | null;            // Scope for accounts: fundraising, advisory, kol_activation, gtm, bd_partnerships, apac
    64→  notes: string | null;
    65→  position: number;
    66→  created_at: string;
    67→  updated_at: string;
    68→  qualified_at: string | null;
    69→  closed_at: string | null;
    70→  // Joined data
    71→  affiliate?: CRMAffiliate | null;
    72→  contacts?: CRMContact[];
    73→  client?: { id: string; name: string } | null;
    74→}
    75→
    76→export interface CRMPartner {
    77→  id: string;
    78→  name: string;
    79→  category: PartnerCategory | null;
    80→  focus: string | null;
    81→  status: PartnerStatus;
    82→  last_contacted_at: string | null;
    83→  owner_id: string | null;
    84→  poc_name: string | null;
    85→  poc_email: string | null;
    86→  poc_telegram: string | null;
    87→  is_affiliate: boolean;
    88→  affiliate_id: string | null;
    89→  notes: string | null;
    90→  created_at: string;
    91→  updated_at: string;
    92→  // Joined data
    93→  affiliate?: CRMAffiliate | null;
    94→  contacts?: CRMContact[];
    95→}
    96→
    97→export interface CRMContact {
    98→  id: string;
    99→  name: string;
   100→  email: string | null;
   101→  telegram_id: string | null;
   102→  x_id: string | null;
   103→  role: string | null;
   104→  category: string | null;
   105→  owner_id: string | null;
   106→  notes: string | null;

   110→

   115→  opportunity_id: string | null;

   120→  created_at: string;
   121→  // Joined data

   124→
   125→export interface CRMStageHistory {

   128→  object_id: string;

```text
     1→import { createBrowserClient } from '@supabase/ssr'
     2→import { Database } from './database.types'
     3→
     4→const supabaseUrl = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL || 'https://placeholder-url.supabase.co'
     5→const supabaseAnonKey = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY || 'placeholder-key'
     6→
     7→// Use createBrowserClient from @supabase/ssr for cookie-based auth
     8→// This allows server-side API routes to read the session from cookies
     9→export const supabase = createBrowserClient<Database>(supabaseUrl, supabaseAnonKey)
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./lib/supabase-server.ts`

```text
     1→import { createServerClient as createSupabaseServerClient } from '@supabase/ssr';
     2→import { cookies } from 'next/headers';
     3→import { Database } from './database.types';
     4→
     5→/**
     6→ * Create a Supabase client for Server Components and API routes
     7→ * This client can access cookies and retrieve the user session
     8→ */
     9→export async function createServerClient() {
    10→  const cookieStore = await cookies();
    11→  const supabaseUrl = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL!;
    12→  const supabaseAnonKey = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY!;
    13→
    14→  return createSupabaseServerClient<Database>(
    15→    supabaseUrl,
    16→    supabaseAnonKey,
    17→    {
    18→      cookies: {
    19→        getAll() {
    20→          return cookieStore.getAll();
    21→        },
    22→        setAll(cookiesToSet) {
    23→          try {
    24→            cookiesToSet.forEach(({ name, value, options }) =>
    25→              cookieStore.set(name, value, options)
    26→            );
    27→          } catch {
    28→            // The `setAll` method was called from a Server Component.
    29→            // This can be ignored if you have middleware refreshing user sessions.
    30→          }
    31→        },
    32→      },
    33→    }
    34→  );
    35→}
    36→
```
</details></tool-use>

   196→
   197→// ============================================
   198→// CRM Service Class
   199→// ============================================
   200→
   201→export class CRMService {
   202→  // ----------------------------------------
   203→  // AFFILIATES
   204→  // ----------------------------------------
   205→
   206→  static async getAllAffiliates(): Promise<CRMAffiliate[]> {
   207→    const { data, error } = await supabase
   208→      .from('crm_affiliates')
   209→      .select('*')
   210→      .order('created_at', { ascending: false });
   211→
   212→    if (error) throw error;
   213→    return data || [];
   214→  }
   215→
   216→  static async getAffiliateById(id: string): Promise<CRMAffiliate | null> {
   217→    const { data, error } = await supabase
   218→      .from('crm_affiliates')
   219→      .select('*')
   220→      .eq('id', id)
   221→      .single();
   222→
   223→    if (error) throw error;
   224→    return data;
   225→  }
   226→
   227→  static async createAffiliate(affiliateData: CreateAffiliateData): Promise<CRMAffiliate> {
   228→    const { data, error } = await supabase
   229→      .from('crm_affiliates')
   230→      .insert([affiliateData])
   231→      .select()
   232→      .single();
   233→
   234→    if (error) throw error;
   235→
   236→    // Record stage history
   237→    await this.recordStageHistory('affiliate', data.id, null, affiliateData.status || 'new');
   238→
   239→    return data;
   240→  }
   241→
   242→  static async updateAffiliate(id: string, updates: Partial<CreateAffiliateData>): Promise<CRMAffiliate> {
   243→    // Get current state for stage history
   244→    const current = await this.getAffiliateById(id);
   245→
   246→    const { data, error } = await supabase
   247→      .from('crm_affiliates')
   248→      .update({ ...updates, updated_at: new Date().toISOString() })
   249→      .eq('id', id)
   250→      .select()
   251→      .single();
   252→
   253→    if (error) throw error;
   254→
   255→    // Record stage change if status changed
   256→    if (updates.status && current && current.status !== updates.status) {
   257→      await this.recordStageHistory('affiliate', id, current.status, updates.status);
   258→    }
   259→
   260→    return data;
   261→  }
   262→
   263→  static async deleteAffiliate(id: string): Promise<void> {
   264→    const { error } = await supabase
   265→      .from('crm_affiliates')
   266→      .delete()
   267→      .eq('id', id);
   268→
   269→    if (error) throw error;
   270→  }
   271→
   272→  // ----------------------------------------
   273→  // OPPORTUNITIES
   274→  // ----------------------------------------
   275→
   276→  static async getAllOpportunities(): Promise<CRMOpportunity[]> {
   277→    const { data, error } = await supabase
   278→      .from('crm_opportunities')
   279→      .select(`
   280→        *,
   281→        affiliate:crm_affiliates(*),
   282→        client:clients!crm_opportunities_client_id_fkey(id, name)
   283→      `)
   284→      .order('position', { ascending: true })
   285→      .order('created_at', { ascending: false });
   286→
   287→    if (error) {
   288→      console.error('Error fetching opportunities:', error);
   289→      throw error;
   290→    }
   291→    return data || [];
   292→  }
   293→
   294→  static async getOpportunitiesByStage(stages: OpportunityStage[]): Promise<CRMOpportunity[]> {
   295→    const { data, error } = await supabase
   296→      .from('crm_opportunities')
   297→      .select(`
   298→        *,
   299→        affiliate:crm_affiliates(*),
   300→        client:clients!crm_opportunities_client_id_fkey(id, name)
   301→      `)
   302→      .in('stage', stages)
   303→      .order('created_at', { ascending: false });
   304→
   305→    if (error) throw error;
   306→    return data || [];
   307→  }
   308→
   309→  static async getOpportunityById(id: string): Promise<CRMOpportunity | null> {
   310→    const { data, error } = await supabase
   311→      .from('crm_opportunities')
   312→      .select(`
   313→        *,
   314→        affiliate:crm_affiliates(*),
   315→        client:clients!crm_opportunities_client_id_fkey(id, name)
   316→      `)
   317→      .eq('id', id)
   318→      .single();
   319→
   320→    if (error) throw error;
   321→    return data;
   322→  }
   323→
   324→  static async createOpportunity(opportunityData: CreateOpportunityData): Promise<CRMOpportunity> {
   325→    const { data, error } = await supabase
   326→      .from('crm_opportunities')
   327→      .insert([opportunityData])
   328→      .select()
   329→      .single();
   330→
   331→    if (error) throw error;
   332→
   333→    // Record stage history
   334→    await this.recordStageHistory('opportunity', data.id, null, opportunityData.stage || 'new');
   335→
   336→    return data;
   337→  }
   338→
   339→  static async updateOpportunity(id: string, updates: Partial<CreateOpportunityData>): Promise<CRMOpportunity> {
   340→    // Get current state for stage history
   341→    const current = await this.getOpportunityById(id);
   342→
   343→    const updateData: any = { ...updates, updated_at: new Date().toISOString() };
   344→
   345→    // Set qualified_at when moving to deal stages
   346→    if (updates.stage && ['proposal', 'contract', 'closed_won', 'closed_lost'].includes(updates.stage)) {
   347→      if (current && !current.qualified_at && ['new', 'contacted', 'qualified', 'unqualified', 'nurture'].includes(current.stage)) {
   348→        updateData.qualified_at = new Date().toISOString();
   349→      }
   350→    }
   351→
   352→    // Set closed_at when closing
   353→    if (updates.stage && ['closed_won', 'closed_lost'].includes(updates.stage)) {
   354→      if (current && !current.closed_at) {
   355→        updateData.closed_at = new Date().toISOString();
   356→      }
   357→    }
   358→
   359→    const { data, error } = await supabase
   360→      .from('crm_opportunities')
   361→      .update(updateData)
   362→      .eq('id', id)
   363→      .select()
   364→      .single();
   365→
   366→    if (error) throw error;
   367→
   368→    // Record stage change if stage changed
   369→    if (updates.stage && current && current.stage !== updates.stage) {
   370→      await this.recordStageHistory('opportunity', id, current.stage, updates.stage);
   371→    }
   372→
   373→    return data;
   374→  }
   375→
   376→  static async deleteOpportunity(id: string): Promise<void> {
   377→    const { error } = await supabase
   378→      .from('crm_opportunities')
   379→      .delete()
   380→      .eq('id', id);
   381→
   382→    if (error) throw error;
   383→  }
   384→
   385→  static async updateOpportunityPositions(positions: { id: string; position: number }[]): Promise<void> {
   386→    // Update each opportunity's position
   387→    const updates = positions.map(({ id, position }) =>
   388→      supabase
   389→        .from('crm_opportunities')
   390→        .update({ position, updated_at: new Date().toISOString() })
   391→        .eq('id', id)
   392→    );
   393→
   394→    const results = await Promise.all(updates);
   395→    const errors = results.filter(r => r.error);
   396→    if (errors.length > 0) {
   397→      console.error('Errors updating positions:', errors);
   398→      throw errors[0].error;
   399→    }
```
</details></tool-use>

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 2026-03-17 10:21:35Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./lib/crmService.ts`

```text
   650→    if (error) throw error;
   651→    return data || [];
   652→  }
   653→
   654→  // ----------------------------------------
   655→  // STAGE HISTORY
   656→  // ----------------------------------------
   657→
   658→  static async recordStageHistory(
   659→    objectType: 'opportunity' | 'partner' | 'affiliate',
   660→    objectId: string,
   661→    fromStage: string | null,
   662→    toStage: string,
   663→    notes?: string
   664→  ): Promise<void> {
   665→    const { data: { user } } = await supabase.auth.getUser();
   666→
   667→    const { error } = await supabase
   668→      .from('crm_stage_history')
   669→      .insert([{
   670→        object_type: objectType,
   671→        object_id: objectId,
   672→        from_stage: fromStage,
   673→        to_stage: toStage,
   674→        changed_by: user?.id || null,
   675→        notes
   676→      }]);
   677→
   678→    if (error) {
   679→      console.error('Error recording stage history:', error);
   680→      // Don't throw - stage history is non-critical
   681→    }
   682→  }
   683→
   684→  static async getStageHistory(objectType: string, objectId: string): Promise<CRMStageHistory[]> {
   685→    const { data, error } = await supabase
   686→      .from('crm_stage_history')
   687→      .select('*')
   688→      .eq('object_type', objectType)
   689→      .eq('object_id', objectId)
   690→      .order('changed_at', { ascending: false });
   691→
   692→    if (error) throw error;
   693→    return data || [];
   694→  }
   695→
   696→  // ----------------------------------------
   697→  // UTILITY METHODS
   698→  // ----------------------------------------
   699→
   700→  static async updateLastContacted(
   701→    type: 'opportunity' | 'partner' | 'affiliate',
   702→    id: string
   703→  ): Promise<void> {
   704→    const tableName = type === 'opportunity' ? 'crm_opportunities' :
   705→                      type === 'partner' ? 'crm_partners' : 'crm_affiliates';
   706→
   707→    const { error } = await supabase
   708→      .from(tableName)
   709→      .update({
   710→        last_contacted_at: new Date().toISOString(),
   711→        updated_at: new Date().toISOString()
   712→      })
   713→      .eq('id', id);
   714→
   715→    if (error) throw error;
   716→  }
   717→
   718→  // ----------------------------------------
   719→  // STATIC OPTIONS
   720→  // ----------------------------------------
   721→
   722→  static getOpportunityStages() {
   723→    return {
   724→      lead: [
   725→        { value: 'new', label: 'New' },
   726→        { value: 'contacted', label: 'Contacted' },
   727→        { value: 'qualified', label: 'Qualified' },
   728→        { value: 'unqualified', label: 'Unqualified' },
   729→        { value: 'nurture', label: 'Nurture' },

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 2026-03-17 10:21:53Z)**_

Perfect! Now I have enough information. Let me create a comprehensive summary report:

## Service Layer Pattern Analysis Report

Based on my analysis of the KOL Campaign Manager project, here's the complete breakdown of the service layer architecture:

### ClientService (Representative Service File)

Full content available in `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/lib/clientService.ts` (310 lines):

**Key Patterns Identified:**

#### 1. **Supabase Import & Client Setup**
```typescript
import { supabase } from './supabase';
import { Database } from './database.types';
```
- Uses a singleton browser client from `/lib/supabase.ts`
- Browser client created with `createBrowserClient<Database>()` from `@supabase/ssr`
- Includes TypeScript-generated database types from `database.types.ts`

#### 2. **Service Architecture**
- **Static class pattern**: All methods are static (no instantiation needed)
- **Modular methods**: Each method handles a single responsibility
- **Type definitions**: Custom interfaces for enhanced type safety (e.g., `ClientWithAccess` extends `Client`)
- **No constructor or state management** - purely functional wrapper around Supabase

#### 3. **Method Structure (Consistent Pattern)**
```typescript
static async methodName(params): Promise<ReturnType> {
  try {
    const { data, error } = await supabase
      .from('table_name')
      .operation()
      .conditions();
    
    if (error) throw error;
    return data || [];
  } catch (error) {
    console.error('Error message:', error);
    throw error;
  }
}
```

#### 4. **Error Handling**
- Check for Supabase `error` object after every query
- Throw errors to caller
- Console.error for debugging
- Some services (like CRMService) use non-critical error handling for auxiliary operations

#### 5. **Data Type Extraction**
```typescript
type Client = Database['public']['Tables']['clients']['Row'];
type ClientAccessMember = Database['public']['Tables']['client_access_members']['Row'];
```
- Extracts Row types from generated database types
- Prevents manual type duplication
- Ensures types always match database schema

#### 6. **Query Patterns in ClientService:**
- **Read operations**: `.select()` with joins using backtick notation
- **Count operations**: `table(count)` for aggregation without fetching full rows
- **Filtering**: `.eq()`, `.is()`, `.in()` for conditional queries
- **Ordering**: `.order('field', { ascending: boolean })`
- **Joins**: Nested selection with foreign key references
- **Single vs Multiple**: `.single()` for single record, array return for multiple

#### 7. **Computed/Enhanced Types**
ClientService returns `ClientWithAccess` which enriches the base `Client` type:
```typescript
export interface ClientWithAccess extends Client {
  member_count?: number;
  campaign_count?: number;
  whitelist_partner_name?: string;
}
```
- Maps aggregated counts from Supabase joins to properties
- Denormalizes related data (whitelist partner name)

---

### CRMService (Comparison Example)

Located at `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/lib/crmService.ts` - demonstrates more advanced patterns:

**Additional Patterns:**

1. **Stage-based entities**: Opportunities, Partners, Affiliates all track stages
2. **Audit trail**: `recordStageHistory()` tracks all stage transitions
3. **Complex joins**: Multi-table selects with aliases:
   ```typescript
   .select(`
     *,
     affiliate:crm_affiliates(*),
     client:clients!crm_opportunities_client_id_fkey(id, name)
   `)
   ```

4. **Business logic in service**: Sets `qualified_at`, `closed_at` timestamps automatically on stage changes
5. **Batch operations**: `updateOpportunityPositions()` uses `Promise.all()`
6. **Utility methods**: `updateLastContacted()`, `getOpportunityStages()` (static option builders)
7. **Non-critical error handling**: Stage history recording doesn't throw (logged but continues)

---

### Database Type System

Generated in `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/lib/database.types.ts`:

**Type Structure (3 variants per table):**
- `Row`: Represents a database record (read-only)
- `Insert`: Defines required/optional fields for creation
- `Update`: All fields optional for partial updates
- **No Task table exists** - project manages tasks through campaigns, contents, and opportunity stages

**Tables in this project:**
- Core: `clients`, `campaigns`, `campaign_kols`, `contents`, `users`
- CRM: `crm_opportunities`, `crm_partners`, `crm_affiliates`, `crm_contacts`, `crm_contact_links`, `crm_stage_history`
- Templates: `campaign_templates`, `campaign_template_budget_allocations`
- Messaging: `message_templates`, `chat_sessions`, `chat_messages`
- Tracking: `payments`, `campaign_updates`, `campaign_report_files`
- Lists: `lists`, `list_kols`
- KOLs: `master_kols`

---

### Key Files Summary

| File | Purpose | Key Pattern |
|------|---------|-------------|
| `/lib/supabase.ts` | Browser client singleton | `createBrowserClient<Database>()` |
| `/lib/supabase-server.ts` | Server-side client factory | Async function returning cookie-aware client |
| `/lib/clientService.ts` | Client CRUD + access control | Role-based queries, multi-join selection |
| `/lib/crmService.ts` | CRM entity management | Static CRUD per entity, stage tracking, audit trail |
| `/lib/database.types.ts` | Generated TypeScript definitions | Database schema as types (32KB file) |

**No task-related interfaces exist separately** - the project uses existing domain models (campaigns, contents, opportunities) to manage work.

   129→
   130→const getDueDateColor = (dueDate: string | null) => {
   131→  if (!dueDate) return '';
   132→  const today = new Date();
   133→  today.setHours(0, 0, 0, 0);
   134→  const due = new Date(dueDate + 'T00:00:00');
   135→  const diffDays = Math.ceil((due.getTime() - today.getTime()) / (1000 * 60 * 60 * 24));
   136→  if (diffDays < 0) return 'text-red-600 font-semibold';
   137→  if (diffDays <= 3) return 'text-amber-600 font-semibold';
   138→  return 'text-gray-600';
   139→};
   140→
   141→/** Format a Date to YYYY-MM-DD in local timezone (avoids UTC offset issues) */
   142→const toLocalDateString = (date: Date) => {
   143→  const year = date.getFullYear();
   144→  const month = String(date.getMonth() + 1).padStart(2, '0');
   145→  const day = String(date.getDate()).padStart(2, '0');
   146→  return `${year}-${month}-${day}`;
   147→};

   304→  // Filter tasks by tab + search
   305→  const filtered = useMemo(() => {
   306→    let tabFiltered: Task[];
   307→    if (activeTab === 'one-time') {
   308→      tabFiltered = tasks.filter(t => t.frequency === 'one-time' && t.task_type !== 'Client SOP');
   309→    } else if (activeTab === 'recurring') {
   310→      tabFiltered = tasks.filter(t => t.frequency !== 'one-time' && t.task_type !== 'Client SOP');
   311→    } else {
   312→      tabFiltered = tasks.filter(t => t.task_type === 'Client SOP');
   313→    }
   314→
   315→    if (!searchTerm) return tabFiltered.sort((a, b) => a.sort_order - b.sort_order);
   316→
   317→    const term = searchTerm.toLowerCase();
   318→    return tabFiltered.filter(t =>
   319→      t.task_name.toLowerCase().includes(term) ||
   320→      (t.assigned_to_name && t.assigned_to_name.toLowerCase().includes(term)) ||
   321→      (t.latest_comment && t.latest_comment.toLowerCase().includes(term)) ||
   322→      t.task_type.toLowerCase().includes(term) ||
   323→      (t.created_by_name && t.created_by_name.toLowerCase().includes(term))
   324→    ).sort((a, b) => a.sort_order - b.sort_order);
   325→  }, [tasks, activeTab, searchTerm]);
   326→
   327→  // Group filtered tasks by assigned_to
   328→  const groupedByUser = useMemo(() => {
   329→    const groups: { key: string; label: string; tasks: Task[] }[] = [];
   330→    const map = new Map<string, Task[]>();
   331→
   332→    for (const task of filtered) {
   333→      const key = task.assigned_to || '_unassigned';
   334→      if (!map.has(key)) map.set(key, []);
   335→      map.get(key)!.push(task);
   336→    }
   337→
   338→    const sortedKeys = Array.from(map.keys()).sort((a, b) => {
   339→      if (a === '_unassigned') return 1;
   340→      if (b === '_unassigned') return -1;
   341→      const nameA = map.get(a)![0].assigned_to_name || '';
   342→      const nameB = map.get(b)![0].assigned_to_name || '';
   343→      return nameA.localeCompare(nameB);
   344→    });
   345→
   346→    for (const key of sortedKeys) {
   347→      const tasks = map.get(key)!;
   348→      const label = key === '_unassigned' ? 'Unassigned' : (tasks[0].assigned_to_name || 'Unknown');
   349→      groups.push({ key, label, tasks });
   350→    }
   351→
   352→    return groups;
   353→  }, [filtered]);
   354→
   355→  const toggleUserCollapse = (key: string) => {
   356→    setCollapsedUsers(prev => {
   357→      const newSet = new Set(prev);
   358→      if (newSet.has(key)) newSet.delete(key);
   359→      else newSet.add(key);
   360→      return newSet;
   361→    });
   362→  };
   363→
   364→  // --- Inline editing ---
   365→  const startEditing = (taskId: string, field: string, currentValue: string) => {

   368→  };
   369→
   370→  const cancelEditing = () => {

   374→

   384→        [field]: value,
   385→        updated_at: new Date().toISOString(),

   389→      await fetchTasks();
   390→    }
   391→  };
   392→
   393→  const saveSelectField = async (taskId: string, field: string, value: string, extra?: Record<string, string | null>) => {

   395→    setTasks(prev => prev.map(t => t.id === taskId ? { ...t, ...updates, updated_at: new Date().toISOString() } : t));

   397→      await supabase.from('tasks').update({
   398→        ...updates,
   399→        updated_at: new Date().toISOString(),
   400→      }).eq('id', taskId);
   401→    } catch (error) {
   402→      console.error('Error saving:', error);
   403→      await fetchTasks();
   404→    }
   405→  };
   406→
   407→  const saveDateField = async (taskId: string, date: Date | undefined) => {
   408→    if (!date) return;
   409→    const dateStr = toLocalDateString(date);
   410→    setEditingCell(null);
   411→    setEditingValue('');
   412→    setTasks(prev => prev.map(t => t.id === taskId ? { ...t, due_date: dateStr, updated_at: new Date().toISOString() } : t));
   413→    try {
   414→      await supabase.from('tasks').update({

   416→        updated_at: new Date().toISOString(),
   417→      }).eq('id', taskId);
   418→    } catch (error) {

   420→      await fetchTasks();
   421→    }
   422→  };
   423→
   424→  const openForm = (task?: Task) => {

   507→  const handleDelete = async (id: string) => {
   508→    setDeletingId(null);
   509→    setTasks(prev => prev.filter(t => t.id !== id));
   510→    try {
   511→      await supabase.from('tasks').delete().eq('id', id);

   517→
   518→  // Global sorted list of all filtered tasks (used for reordering across groups)
   519→  const globalSorted = useMemo(() =>
   520→    [...filtered].sort((a, b) => a.sort_order - b.sort_order),
   521→    [filtered]
   522→  );
   523→
   524→  const handleReorder = async (taskId: string, direction: 'up' | 'down') => {
   525→    const idx = globalSorted.findIndex(t => t.id === taskId);
   526→    const swapIdx = direction === 'up' ? idx - 1 : idx + 1;
   527→    if (swapIdx < 0 || swapIdx >= globalSorted.length) return;
   528→
   529→    const current = globalSorted[idx];
   530→    const swap = globalSorted[swapIdx];
   531→
   532→    const currentOrder = current.sort_order;
   533→    const swapOrder = swap.sort_order;
   534→    const newCurrentOrder = currentOrder === swapOrder
   535→      ? (direction === 'up' ? swapOrder - 1 : swapOrder + 1)
   536→      : swapOrder;
   537→    const newSwapOrder = currentOrder === swapOrder
   538→      ? currentOrder
   539→      : currentOrder;
   540→
   541→    setTasks(prev => prev.map(t => {
   542→      if (t.id === current.id) return { ...t, sort_order: newCurrentOrder };
   543→      if (t.id === swap.id) return { ...t, sort_order: newSwapOrder };
   544→      return t;
   545→    }));
   546→
   547→    try {
   548→      await Promise.all([
   549→        supabase.from('tasks').update({ sort_order: newCurrentOrder }).eq('id', current.id),
   550→        supabase.from('tasks').update({ sort_order: newSwapOrder }).eq('id', swap.id),
   551→      ]);
   552→    } catch (error) {
   553→      console.error('Error reordering:', error);
   554→      await fetchTasks();
   555→    }
   556→  };

   825→        const creatorName = task.created_by_name || '—';
   826→        return (
   827→          <td key={col} className={`py-3 px-3 whitespace-nowrap ${COL.createdBy}`}>
   828→            {task.created_by_name ? (
   829→              <TooltipProvider delayDuration={0}>
   830→                <Tooltip>
   831→                  <TooltipTrigger asChild>
   832→                    <div className="flex items-center cursor-default">
   833→                      {creatorPhoto ? (
   834→                        <div className="h-6 w-6 rounded-full overflow-hidden flex-shrink-0">
   835→                          <img src={creatorPhoto} alt={creatorName} className="w-full h-full object-cover" />
   836→                        </div>
   837→                      ) : (
   838→                        <div className="h-6 w-6 rounded-full bg-gradient-to-br from-[#3e8692] to-[#2d6470] flex items-center justify-center text-white text-[10px] font-bold flex-shrink-0">
   839→                          {getUserInitials(creatorName)}
   840→                        </div>
   841→                      )}
   842→                    </div>
   843→                  </TooltipTrigger>
   844→                  <TooltipContent side="top" className="text-xs">
   845→                    {creatorName}
   846→                  </TooltipContent>
   847→                </Tooltip>
   848→              </TooltipProvider>
   849→            ) : (
   850→              <span className="text-gray-400 text-xs">—</span>
   851→            )}
   852→          </td>
   853→        );
   854→      }
   855→      case 'created':
   856→        return (
   857→          <td key={col} className={`py-3 px-3 whitespace-nowrap ${COL.created}`}>
   858→            <span className="text-gray-500 text-xs">
   859→              {new Date(task.created_at).toLocaleDateString('en-US', { month: 'short', day: 'numeric' })}
   860→            </span>
   861→          </td>
   862→        );
   863→    }
   864→  };
   865→
   866→  const renderTaskRow = (task: Task) => {
   867→    const globalIdx = globalSorted.findIndex(t => t.id === task.id);
   868→
   869→    return (
   870→      <tr key={task.id} className="border-b border-gray-100 hover:bg-gray-50/50 transition-colors group">
   871→        {/* Reorder arrows - always first */}
   872→        <td className={`py-3 px-3 ${COL.reorder}`}>
   873→          <div className="flex flex-col items-center gap-0.5">
   874→            <Button
   875→              variant="ghost"
   876→              size="sm"
   877→              className="w-auto h-auto px-1 py-0 hover:bg-gray-100 disabled:opacity-20"
   878→              onClick={() => handleReorder(task.id, 'up')}
   879→              disabled={globalIdx === 0}
   880→            >
   881→              <ChevronUp className="h-3.5 w-3.5 text-gray-400" />
   882→            </Button>
   883→            <Button
   884→              variant="ghost"
   885→              size="sm"
   886→              className="w-auto h-auto px-1 py-0 hover:bg-gray-100 disabled:opacity-20"
   887→              onClick={() => handleReorder(task.id, 'down')}
   888→              disabled={globalIdx === globalSorted.length - 1}
   889→            >
   890→              <ChevronDown className="h-3.5 w-3.5 text-gray-400" />
   891→            </Button>
   892→          </div>
   893→        </td>
   894→
   895→        {/* Status dropdown */}
   896→        <td className={`py-3 px-1 ${COL.status}`}>
   897→          <Popover>
   898→            <PopoverTrigger asChild>
   899→              {(() => {
   900→                const cfg = STATUS_CONFIG[task.status] || STATUS_CONFIG.to_do;
   901→                const Icon = cfg.icon;
   902→                return (
   903→                  <button className={`p-1 rounded ${cfg.bg} transition-colors`} title={cfg.label}>
   904→                    <Icon className={`h-4 w-4 ${cfg.color}`} />
   905→                  </button>
   906→                );
   907→              })()}
   908→            </PopoverTrigger>
   909→            <PopoverContent className="w-auto p-1" align="start">
   910→              <div className="flex flex-col gap-0.5">
   911→                {STATUSES.map((s) => {
   912→                  const cfg = STATUS_CONFIG[s];
   913→                  const Icon = cfg.icon;
   914→                  return (
   915→                    <button
   916→                      key={s}
   917→                      className={`flex items-center gap-2 px-3 py-1.5 rounded text-sm hover:bg-gray-100 transition-colors text-left ${task.status === s ? 'bg-gray-100 font-medium' : ''}`}
   918→                      onClick={() => saveSelectField(task.id, 'status', s)}
   919→                    >
   920→                      <Icon className={`h-4 w-4 ${cfg.color}`} />
   921→                      {cfg.label}
   922→                    </button>
   923→                  );
   924→                })}
   925→              </div>
   926→            </PopoverContent>
   927→          </Popover>
   928→        </td>
   929→
   930→        {/* Dynamic columns based on columnOrder */}
   931→        {columnOrder.map((col) => renderCell(task, col))}
   932→
   933→        {/* Actions - always last */}
   934→        <td className={`py-3 px-3 text-right ${COL.actions}`}>
   935→          <div className="flex items-center justify-end gap-0.5 opacity-0 group-hover:opacity-100 transition-opacity">
   936→            <Button variant="ghost" size="sm" className="h-7 w-7 p-0 hover:bg-gray-200" onClick={() => openForm(task)} title="Edit in popup">
   937→              <Expand className="h-3.5 w-3.5 text-gray-500" />
   938→            </Button>
   939→            <Button variant="ghost" size="sm" className="h-7 w-7 p-0 hover:bg-red-50" onClick={() => setDeletingId(task.id)}>
   940→              <Trash2 className="h-3.5 w-3.5 text-red-500" />
   941→            </Button>
   942→          </div>
   943→        </td>
   944→      </tr>
   945→    );
   946→  };
   947→
   948→  const renderTaskTable = (groupTasks: Task[], groupKey: string) => {
   949→    const sorted = [...groupTasks].sort((a, b) => a.sort_order - b.sort_order);
   950→    return (
   951→      <table className="w-full text-sm table-fixed">
   952→        {tableHeader}
   953→        <tbody>
   954→          {sorted.map((task) => renderTaskRow(task))}
   955→          {addingToGroup === groupKey && (
   956→            <tr className="border-b border-gray-100 bg-gray-50/30">
   957→              <td className={`py-3 px-3 ${COL.reorder}`}></td>
   958→              <td className={`py-3 px-3 ${COL.taskName}`} colSpan={9}>
   959→                <Input
   960→                  value={newRowName}
   961→                  onChange={(e) => setNewRowName(e.target.value)}
   962→                  onBlur={handleSaveNewRow}
   963→                  onKeyDown={(e) => {
   964→                    if (e.key === 'Enter') handleSaveNewRow();
   965→                    if (e.key === 'Escape') { setAddingToGroup(null); setNewRowName(''); }
   966→                  }}
   967→                  placeholder="Enter task name..."
   968→                  className="w-full border-none shadow-none p-0 h-auto bg-transparent focus:outline-none focus:ring-0 focus-visible:outline-none focus-visible:ring-0 text-sm font-medium"
   969→                  style={{ outline: 'none', boxShadow: 'none' }}
   970→                  autoFocus
   971→                />
   972→              </td>
   973→            </tr>
   974→          )}
   975→        </tbody>
   976→      </table>
   977→    );
   978→  };
   979→
   980→  return (
   981→    <div className="min-h-[calc(100vh-64px)] w-full bg-gray-50">
   982→      <div className="w-full">
   983→        <div className="space-y-4">
   984→          {/* Header */}
   985→          <div className="w-full bg-white border border-gray-200 shadow-sm p-6">
   986→            <div className="pb-5 border-b border-gray-100 flex flex-row items-center justify-between">
   987→              <div className="flex items-center gap-3">
   988→                <div className="bg-gray-100 p-2 rounded-lg">
   989→                  <ListTodo className="h-6 w-6 text-gray-600" />
   990→                </div>
   991→                <div>
   992→                  <h2 className="text-2xl font-bold text-gray-900">Tasks</h2>
   993→                  <p className="text-sm text-gray-500">Manage team tasks, SOPs, and recurring work</p>
   994→                </div>
   995→              </div>
   996→              <Button
   997→                className="hover:opacity-90"
   998→                style={{ backgroundColor: '#3e8692', color: 'white' }}
   999→                onClick={() => openForm()}
  1000→              >
  1001→                <Plus className="h-4 w-4 mr-2" />
  1002→                Add Task
  1003→              </Button>
  1004→            </div>
  1005→
  1006→            {/* Tabs */}
  1007→            <div className="pt-4">
  1008→              {loading ? (
  1009→                <div className="flex gap-2">
  1010→                  {[1, 2, 3].map((i) => <Skeleton key={i} className="h-9 w-28 rounded" />)}
  1011→                </div>
  1012→              ) : (
  1013→                <Tabs value={activeTab} onValueChange={setActiveTab}>
  1014→                  <TabsList className="bg-gray-100 p-1 h-auto flex-wrap">
  1015→                    <TabsTrigger
  1016→                      value="one-time"
  1017→                      className="data-[state=active]:bg-white data-[state=active]:text-[#3e8692] data-[state=active]:shadow-sm text-sm px-4 py-2"
  1018→                    >
  1019→                      One-Time
  1020→                      <span className="ml-2 text-xs bg-gray-200 text-gray-600 px-1.5 py-0.5 rounded-full">{oneTimeCount}</span>
  1021→                    </TabsTrigger>
  1022→                    <TabsTrigger
  1023→                      value="recurring"
  1024→                      className="data-[state=active]:bg-white data-[state=active]:text-[#3e8692] data-[state=active]:shadow-sm text-sm px-4 py-2"
  1025→                    >
  1026→                      Recurring
  1027→                      <span className="ml-2 text-xs bg-gray-200 text-gray-600 px-1.5 py-0.5 rounded-full">{recurringCount}</span>
  1028→                    </TabsTrigger>
  1029→                    <TabsTrigger
  1030→                      value="client-sop"
  1031→                      className="data-[state=active]:bg-white data-[state=active]:text-[#3e8692] data-[state=active]:shadow-sm text-sm px-4 py-2"
  1032→                    >
  1033→                      Client SOP
  1034→                      <span className="ml-2 text-xs bg-gray-200 text-gray-600 px-1.5 py-0.5 rounded-full">{clientSopCount}</span>
  1035→                    </TabsTrigger>
  1036→                  </TabsList>
  1037→                </Tabs>
  1038→              )}
  1039→            </div>
  1040→
  1041→            {/* Search */}
  1042→            <div className="flex flex-wrap items-center gap-3 pt-4">
  1043→              <div className="relative flex-1 max-w-sm">
  1044→                <Search className="absolute left-3 top-1/2 transform -translate-y-1/2 h-4 w-4 text-gray-400" />
  1045→                <Input
  1046→                  placeholder="Search tasks, assignees, comments..."
  1047→                  className="pl-10 auth-input"
  1048→                  value={searchTerm}
  1049→                  onChange={(e) => setSearchTerm(e.target.value)}
  1050→                />
  1051→              </div>
  1052→              {searchTerm && (
  1053→                <Button variant="ghost" size="sm" onClick={() => setSearchTerm('')}>
  1054→                  Clear
  1055→                </Button>
  1056→              )}
  1057→            </div>
  1058→          </div>
  1059→
  1060→          {/* Grouped Tables */}
  1061→          {loading ? (
  1062→            <div className="p-6 space-y-3">
  1063→              {[1, 2, 3, 4].map((i) => <Skeleton key={i} className="h-12 w-full rounded" />)}
  1064→            </div>
  1065→          ) : filtered.length === 0 ? (
  1066→            <div className="w-full bg-white border border-gray-200 shadow-sm">
  1067→              <div className="text-center py-16">
  1068→                <ClipboardList className="h-12 w-12 text-gray-300 mx-auto mb-3" />
  1069→                <p className="text-gray-500 font-medium">
  1070→                  {tasks.length === 0 ? 'No tasks yet.' : 'No tasks match your filters.'}
  1071→                </p>
  1072→                {tasks.length === 0 && (
  1073→                  <Button
  1074→                    className="mt-4 hover:opacity-90"
  1075→                    style={{ backgroundColor: '#3e8692', color: 'white' }}
  1076→                    onClick={() => openForm()}
  1077→                  >
  1078→                    <Plus className="h-4 w-4 mr-2" /> Create Your First Task
  1079→                  </Button>
  1080→                )}
  1081→              </div>
  1082→            </div>
  1083→          ) : (
  1084→            <div className="space-y-4">
  1085→              {groupedByUser.map((group, groupIdx) => {
  1086→                const isCollapsed = collapsedUsers.has(group.key);
  1087→                const colors = group.key === '_unassigned'
  1088→                  ? { bg: 'bg-slate-50', text: 'text-slate-600', border: 'border-slate-200' }
  1089→                  : getColorForIndex(groupIdx);
  1090→                const photoUrl = group.key !== '_unassigned' ? userPhotoMap[group.key] : null;
  1091→
  1092→                return (
  1093→                  <div key={group.key}>
  1094→                    {/* Group Header */}
  1095→                    <div
  1096→                      className={`flex items-center justify-between px-4 py-3 ${colors.bg} ${isCollapsed ? 'rounded-lg' : 'rounded-t-lg'} border ${colors.border} ${isCollapsed ? '' : 'border-b-0'} cursor-pointer select-none transition-all`}
  1097→                      onClick={() => toggleUserCollapse(group.key)}
```

  1299→              <div className="grid gap-2">
  1300→                <Label>Status</Label>
  1301→                <Select value={form.status} onValueChange={(v) => setForm({ ...form, status: v })}>
  1302→                  <SelectTrigger className="auth-input"><SelectValue placeholder="Select status" /></SelectTrigger>
  1303→                  <SelectContent>
  1304→                    {STATUSES.map((s) => {
  1305→                      const cfg = STATUS_CONFIG[s];
  1306→                      const Icon = cfg.icon;
  1307→                      return (
  1308→                        <SelectItem key={s} value={s}>
  1309→                          <div className="flex items-center gap-2">
  1310→                            <Icon className={`h-3.5 w-3.5 ${cfg.color}`} />
  1311→                            {cfg.label}
  1312→                          </div>
  1313→                        </SelectItem>
  1314→                      );
  1315→                    })}
  1316→                  </SelectContent>
  1317→                </Select>
  1318→              </div>
  1319→
  1320→              {/* Link */}
  1321→              <div className="grid gap-2">
  1322→                <Label>Link</Label>
  1323→                <Input
  1324→                  value={form.link}
  1325→                  onChange={(e) => setForm({ ...form, link: e.target.value })}
  1326→                  placeholder="https://..."
  1327→                  className="auth-input"
  1328→                />
  1329→              </div>
  1330→            </div>
  1331→
  1332→            {/* Latest Comment */}
  1333→            <div className="grid gap-2">
  1334→              <Label>Latest Comment</Label>
  1335→              <Textarea
  1336→                value={form.latest_comment}
  1337→                onChange={(e) => setForm({ ...form, latest_comment: e.target.value })}
  1338→                placeholder="Add a comment or note..."
  1339→                className="auth-input"
  1340→                rows={3}
  1341→              />
  1342→            </div>
  1343→          </div>
  1344→          <DialogFooter>
  1345→            <Button variant="outline" onClick={() => { setIsFormOpen(false); setEditingId(null); }}>Cancel</Button>
  1346→            <Button
  1347→              className="hover:opacity-90"
  1348→              style={{ backgroundColor: '#3e8692', color: 'white' }}
  1349→              onClick={handleSubmit}
  1350→              disabled={!form.task_name.trim() || !form.frequency || !form.task_type || submitting}
  1351→            >
  1352→              {submitting ? (
  1353→                <div className="animate-spin rounded-full h-4 w-4 border-b-2 border-white" />
  1354→              ) : editingId ? 'Save Changes' : 'Add Task'}
  1355→            </Button>
  1356→          </DialogFooter>
  1357→        </DialogContent>
  1358→      </Dialog>
  1359→
  1360→      {/* Delete Confirmation */}
  1361→      <Dialog open={!!deletingId} onOpenChange={(open) => { if (!open) setDeletingId(null); }}>
  1362→        <DialogContent className="max-w-md">
  1363→          <DialogHeader>
  1364→            <DialogTitle>Delete Task</DialogTitle>
  1365→            <DialogDescription>Are you sure you want to delete this task? This action cannot be undone.</DialogDescription>
  1366→          </DialogHeader>
  1367→          <DialogFooter>
  1368→            <Button variant="outline" onClick={() => setDeletingId(null)}>Cancel</Button>
  1369→            <Button variant="destructive" onClick={() => deletingId && handleDelete(deletingId)}>Delete</Button>
  1370→          </DialogFooter>
  1371→        </DialogContent>
  1372→      </Dialog>
  1373→    </div>
  1374→  );
  1375→}
  1376→
```
</details></tool-use>
