```text
     1→import { supabase } from './supabase';
     2→import { Database } from './database.types';
     3→import { generateUniqueSlug } from './slugUtils';
     4→
     5→type Client = Database['public']['Tables']['clients']['Row'];
     6→type ClientAccessMember = Database['public']['Tables']['client_access_members']['Row'];
     7→
     8→export interface ClientWithAccess extends Client {
     9→  member_count?: number;
    10→  campaign_count?: number;
    11→  whitelist_partner_name?: string;
    12→}
    13→
    14→export class ClientService {
    15→  /**
    16→   * Get clients based on user role
    17→   * - Admins: See all clients
    18→   * - Members: See only clients they have access to via client_access_members
    19→   */
    20→  static async getClientsForUser(userRole: 'super_admin' | 'admin' | 'member' | 'client', userId: string): Promise<ClientWithAccess[]> {
    21→    try {
    22→      if (userRole === 'admin' || userRole === 'super_admin') {
    23→        // Admins and super admins can see all non-archived clients
    24→        const { data: clients, error } = await supabase
    25→          .from('clients')
    26→          .select(`
    27→            *,
    28→            client_access_members(count),
    29→            campaigns!campaigns_client_id_fkey(count),
    30→            whitelist_partner:partners!clients_whitelist_partner_id_fkey(name)
    31→          `)
    32→          .is('archived_at', null)
    33→          .order('created_at', { ascending: false });
    34→
    35→        if (error) throw error;
    36→
    37→        return clients?.map(client => ({
    38→          ...client,
    39→          member_count: client.client_access_members?.[0]?.count || 0,
    40→          campaign_count: (client as any).campaigns?.[0]?.count || 0,
    41→          whitelist_partner_name: (client.whitelist_partner as any)?.name
    42→        })) || [];
    43→      } else {
    44→        // Members can only see non-archived clients they have access to
    45→        const { data: clientAccess, error } = await supabase
    46→          .from('client_access_members')
    47→          .select(`
    48→            clients!inner(
    49→              id,
    50→              name,
    51→              email,
    52→              location,
    53→              is_active,
    54→              archived_at,
    55→              created_at,
    56→              updated_at,
    57→              logo_url,
    58→              campaigns!campaigns_client_id_fkey(count),
    59→              whitelist_partner:partners!clients_whitelist_partner_id_fkey(name)
    60→            )
    61→          `)
    62→          .eq('user_id', userId);
    63→
    64→        if (error) throw error;
    65→
    66→        return clientAccess?.map(access => {
    67→          const client = (access as any).clients;
    68→          return {
    69→            ...client,
    70→            campaign_count: client.campaigns?.[0]?.count || 0,
    71→            whitelist_partner_name: (client.whitelist_partner as any)?.name
    72→          };
    73→        }).filter(c => c && !c.archived_at) || [];
    74→      }
    75→    } catch (error) {
    76→      console.error('Error fetching clients:', error);
    77→      throw error;
    78→    }
    79→  }
    80→
    81→  /**
    82→   * Get all non-archived clients (admin only)
    83→   */
    84→  static async getAllClients(): Promise<Client[]> {
    85→    try {
    86→      const { data, error } = await supabase
    87→        .from('clients')
    88→        .select('*')
    89→        .is('archived_at', null)
    90→        .order('created_at', { ascending: false });
    91→
    92→      if (error) throw error;
    93→      return data || [];
    94→    } catch (error) {
    95→      console.error('Error fetching all clients:', error);
    96→      throw error;
    97→    }
    98→  }
    99→
   100→  /**
   101→   * Create a new client
   102→   */
   103→  static async createClient(
   104→    name: string,
   105→    email: string,
   106→    location?: string,
   107→    source?: string,
   108→    onboarding_call_held?: boolean,
   109→    onboarding_call_date?: string | null,
   110→    is_whitelisted?: boolean,
   111→    whitelist_partner_id?: string | null
   112→  ): Promise<Client> {
   113→    try {
   114→      // Generate a unique slug from the client name
   115→      const slug = generateUniqueSlug(name);
   116→
   117→      const { data, error } = await supabase
   118→        .from('clients')
   119→        .insert({ name, email, location, source, onboarding_call_held, onboarding_call_date, is_whitelisted, whitelist_partner_id, slug })
   120→        .select()
   121→        .single();
   122→      if (error) throw error;
   123→      return data;
   124→    } catch (error) {
   125→      console.error('Error creating client:', error);
   126→      throw error;
   127→    }
   128→  }
   129→
   130→  /**
   131→   * Update client
   132→   */
   133→  static async updateClient(id: string, updates: Partial<Pick<Client, 'name' | 'email' | 'location' | 'is_active' | 'is_whitelisted' | 'whitelist_partner_id' | 'logo_url' | 'approved_domains'>>): Promise<Client> {
   134→    try {
   135→      const { data, error } = await supabase
   136→        .from('clients')
   137→        .update(updates)
   138→        .eq('id', id)
   139→        .select()
   140→        .single();
   141→
   142→      if (error) throw error;
   143→      return data;
   144→    } catch (error) {
   145→      console.error('Error updating client:', error);
   146→      throw error;
   147→    }
   148→  }
   149→
   150→  /**
   151→   * Grant user access to a client
   152→   */
   153→  static async grantClientAccess(clientId: string, userId: string): Promise<ClientAccessMember> {
   154→    try {
   155→      const { data, error } = await supabase
   156→        .from('client_access_members')
   157→        .insert({ client_id: clientId, user_id: userId })
   158→        .select()
   159→        .single();
   160→
   161→      if (error) throw error;
   162→      return data;
   163→    } catch (error) {
   164→      console.error('Error granting client access:', error);
   165→      throw error;
   166→    }
   167→  }
   168→
   169→  /**
   170→   * Revoke user access from a client
   171→   */
   172→  static async revokeClientAccess(clientId: string, userId: string): Promise<void> {
   173→    try {
   174→      const { error } = await supabase
   175→        .from('client_access_members')
   176→        .delete()
   177→        .eq('client_id', clientId)
   178→        .eq('user_id', userId);
   179→
   180→      if (error) throw error;
   181→    } catch (error) {
   182→      console.error('Error revoking client access:', error);
   183→      throw error;
   184→    }
   185→  }
   186→
   187→  /**
   188→   * Get users with access to a specific client
   189→   */
   190→  static async getClientMembers(clientId: string): Promise<any[]> {
   191→    try {
   192→      const { data, error } = await supabase
   193→        .from('client_access_members')
   194→        .select(`
   195→          *,
   196→          users!inner(id, name, email, role)
   197→        `)
   198→        .eq('client_id', clientId);
   199→
   200→      if (error) throw error;
   201→      return data?.map(access => access.users) || [];
   202→    } catch (error) {
   203→      console.error('Error fetching client members:', error);
   204→      throw error;
   205→    }
   206→  }
   207→
   208→  /**
   209→   * Get client by ID or slug (for portal access)
   210→   */
   211→  static async getClientByIdOrSlug(identifier: string): Promise<Client | null> {
   212→    try {
   213→      // Check if identifier is a UUID
   214→      const isUUID = /^[0-9a-f]{8}-[0-9a-f]{4}-[0-9a-f]{4}-[0-9a-f]{4}-[0-9a-f]{12}$/i.test(identifier);
   215→
   216→      const query = supabase
   217→        .from('clients')
   218→        .select('*')
   219→        .is('archived_at', null);
   220→
   221→      if (isUUID) {
   222→        query.eq('id', identifier);
   223→      } else {
   224→        query.eq('slug', identifier);
   225→      }
   226→
   227→      const { data, error } = await query.single();
   228→
   229→      if (error) {
   230→        if (error.code === 'PGRST116') return null; // Not found
   231→        throw error;
   232→      }
   233→      return data;
   234→    } catch (error) {
   235→      console.error('Error fetching client by ID or slug:', error);
   236→      throw error;
   237→    }
   238→  }
   239→
   240→  /**
   241→   * Get client with their campaigns for portal display
   242→   */
   243→  static async getClientWithCampaigns(clientId: string): Promise<{
   244→    client: Client;
   245→    campaigns: any[];
   246→  } | null> {
   247→    try {
   248→      // Get client
   249→      const { data: client, error: clientError } = await supabase
   250→        .from('clients')
   251→        .select('*')
   252→        .eq('id', clientId)
   253→        .is('archived_at', null)
   254→        .single();
   255→
   256→      if (clientError || !client) return null;
   257→
   258→      // Get campaigns for this client with KOL and content counts
   259→      const { data: campaigns, error: campaignsError } = await supabase
   260→        .from('campaigns')
   261→        .select(`
   262→          id,
   263→          name,
   264→          slug,
   265→          status,
   266→          total_budget,
   267→          start_date,
   268→          end_date,
   269→          region,
   270→          description,
   271→          share_report_publicly,
   272→          created_at,
   273→          campaign_kols(count),
   274→          contents(
   275→            impressions,
   276→            likes,
   277→            comments,
   278→            retweets,
   279→            bookmarks
   280→          )
   281→        `)
   282→        .eq('client_id', clientId)
   283→        .is('archived_at', null)
   284→        .order('start_date', { ascending: false });
   285→
   286→      if (campaignsError) throw campaignsError;
   287→
   288→      // Process campaigns to add aggregated metrics
   289→      const processedCampaigns = campaigns?.map(campaign => {
   290→        const contents = (campaign as any).contents || [];
   291→        const totalImpressions = contents.reduce((sum: number, c: any) => sum + (c.impressions || 0), 0);
   292→        const totalEngagement = contents.reduce((sum: number, c: any) =>
   293→          sum + (c.likes || 0) + (c.comments || 0) + (c.retweets || 0) + (c.bookmarks || 0), 0);
   294→
   295→        return {
   296→          ...campaign,
   297→          kol_count: (campaign as any).campaign_kols?.[0]?.count || 0,
   298→          content_count: contents.length,
   299→          total_impressions: totalImpressions,
   300→          total_engagement: totalEngagement,
   301→        };
   302→      }) || [];
   303→
   304→      return { client, campaigns: processedCampaigns };
   305→    } catch (error) {
   306→      console.error('Error fetching client with campaigns:', error);
   307→      throw error;
   308→    }
   309→  }
   310→}
```

```typescript
import { supabase } from './supabase';

export type Task = {
  id: string;
  task_name: string;
  assigned_to: string | null;
  assigned_to_name: string | null;
  due_date: string | null;
  latest_comment: string | null;
  frequency: string;
  task_type: string;
  link: string | null;
  description: string | null;
  status: string;
  created_by: string | null;
  created_by_name: string | null;
  sort_order: number;
  created_at: string;
  updated_at: string;
  // New M1 columns
  client_id: string | null;
  parent_task_id: string | null;
  priority: string;
  completed_at: string | null;
  recurring_config: Record<string, any> | null;
  template_id: string | null;
};

export type TaskInsert = Omit<Task, 'id' | 'created_at' | 'updated_at' | 'sort_order'> & {
  sort_order?: number;
};

export type TaskUpdate = Partial<Omit<Task, 'id' | 'created_at'>>;

export class TaskService {
  /**
   * Get all tasks ordered by sort_order then created_at
   */
  static async getAllTasks(): Promise<Task[]> {
    try {
      const { data, error } = await supabase
        .from('tasks')
        .select('*')
        .order('sort_order', { ascending: true })
        .order('created_at', { ascending: false });

      if (error) throw error;
      return (data as Task[]) || [];
    } catch (error) {
      console.error('Error fetching tasks:', error);
      throw error;
    }
  }

  /**
   * Get a single task by ID
   */
  static async getTaskById(id: string): Promise<Task | null> {
    try {
      const { data, error } = await supabase
        .from('tasks')
        .select('*')
        .eq('id', id)
        .single();

      if (error) {
        if (error.code === 'PGRST116') return null;
        throw error;
      }
      return data as Task;
    } catch (error) {
      console.error('Error fetching task:', error);
      throw error;
    }
  }

  /**
   * Create a new task
   */
  static async createTask(task: Partial<TaskInsert>): Promise<Task> {
    try {
      const { data, error } = await supabase
        .from('tasks')
        .insert(task)
        .select()
        .single();

      if (error) throw error;
      return data as Task;
    } catch (error) {
      console.error('Error creating task:', error);
      throw error;
    }
  }

  /**
   * Update a task. Automatically sets completed_at on status change.
   */
  static async updateTask(id: string, updates: TaskUpdate): Promise<Task> {
    try {
      // Handle completed_at logic on status change
      const payload: Record<string, any> = {
        ...updates,
        updated_at: new Date().toISOString(),
      };

      if (updates.status === 'complete' && !updates.completed_at) {
        payload.completed_at = new Date().toISOString();
      } else if (updates.status && updates.status !== 'complete') {
        payload.completed_at = null;
      }

      const { data, error } = await supabase
        .from('tasks')
        .update(payload)
        .eq('id', id)
        .select()
        .single();

      if (error) throw error;
      return data as Task;
    } catch (error) {
      console.error('Error updating task:', error);
      throw error;
    }
  }

  /**
   * Delete a task
   */
  static async deleteTask(id: string): Promise<void> {
    try {
      const { error } = await supabase
        .from('tasks')
        .delete()
        .eq('id', id);

      if (error) throw error;
    } catch (error) {
      console.error('Error deleting task:', error);
      throw error;
    }
  }

  /**
   * Update a single field on a task (for inline edits).
   * Handles completed_at logic for status changes.
   */
  static async updateField(id: string, field: string, value: any): Promise<void> {
    try {
      const payload: Record<string, any> = {
        [field]: value,
        updated_at: new Date().toISOString(),
      };

      // Handle completed_at on status changes
      if (field === 'status' && value === 'complete') {
        payload.completed_at = new Date().toISOString();
      } else if (field === 'status' && value !== 'complete') {
        payload.completed_at = null;
      }

      const { error } = await supabase
        .from('tasks')
        .update(payload)
        .eq('id', id);

      if (error) throw error;
    } catch (error) {
      console.error('Error updating task field:', error);
      throw error;
    }
  }

  /**
   * Reorder tasks by updating sort_order for multiple tasks
   */
  static async reorderTasks(moves: { id: string; sort_order: number }[]): Promise<void> {
    try {
      await Promise.all(
        moves.map(({ id, sort_order }) =>
          supabase.from('tasks').update({ sort_order }).eq('id', id)
        )
      );
    } catch (error) {
      console.error('Error reordering tasks:', error);
      throw error;
    }
  }

  /**
   * Get tasks assigned to a specific user
   */
  static async getTasksForUser(userId: string): Promise<Task[]> {
    try {
      const { data, error } = await supabase
        .from('tasks')
        .select('*')
        .eq('assigned_to', userId)
        .order('sort_order', { ascending: true })
        .order('created_at', { ascending: false });

      if (error) throw error;
      return (data as Task[]) || [];
    } catch (error) {
      console.error('Error fetching tasks for user:', error);
      throw error;
    }
  }

  /**
   * Get tasks linked to a specific client
   */
  static async getTasksForClient(clientId: string): Promise<Task[]> {
    try {
      const { data, error } = await supabase
        .from('tasks')
        .select('*')
        .eq('client_id', clientId)
        .order('sort_order', { ascending: true })
        .order('created_at', { ascending: false });

      if (error) throw error;
      return (data as Task[]) || [];
    } catch (error) {
      console.error('Error fetching tasks for client:', error);
      throw error;
    }
  }

  /**
   * Get subtasks of a parent task
   */
  static async getSubtasks(parentTaskId: string): Promise<Task[]> {
    try {
      const { data, error } = await supabase
        .from('tasks')
        .select('*')
        .eq('parent_task_id', parentTaskId)
        .order('sort_order', { ascending: true });

      if (error) throw error;
      return (data as Task[]) || [];
    } catch (error) {
      console.error('Error fetching subtasks:', error);
      throw error;
    }
  }

  /**
   * Get overdue tasks (due_date < today, not complete)
   */
  static async getOverdueTasks(): Promise<Task[]> {
    try {
      const today = new Date().toISOString().split('T')[0];
      const { data, error } = await supabase
        .from('tasks')
        .select('*')
        .lt('due_date', today)
        .neq('status', 'complete')
        .order('due_date', { ascending: true });

      if (error) throw error;
      return (data as Task[]) || [];
    } catch (error) {
      console.error('Error fetching overdue tasks:', error);
      throw error;
    }
  }

  /**
   * Get tasks due within the next N days (not complete)
   */
  static async getTasksDueSoon(days: number = 7): Promise<Task[]> {
    try {
      const today = new Date();
      const futureDate = new Date(today);
      futureDate.setDate(futureDate.getDate() + days);

      const todayStr = today.toISOString().split('T')[0];
      const futureStr = futureDate.toISOString().split('T')[0];

      const { data, error } = await supabase
        .from('tasks')
        .select('*')
        .gte('due_date', todayStr)
        .lte('due_date', futureStr)
        .neq('status', 'complete')
        .order('due_date', { ascending: true });

      if (error) throw error;
      return (data as Task[]) || [];
    } catch (error) {
      console.error('Error fetching tasks due soon:', error);
      throw error;
    }
  }
}

```

**Milestone 1 complete.** Here's what was done:

1. **DB Migration** (`026_tasks_schema_additions`): Added 6 new columns to `tasks` table:
   - `client_id` (UUID, FK → clients)
   - `parent_task_id` (UUID, self-referencing FK for subtasks)
   - `priority` (TEXT, default 'medium')
   - `completed_at` (TIMESTAMPTZ)
   - `recurring_config` (JSONB)
   - `template_id` (UUID)
   - Plus 5 performance indexes

2. **`lib/taskService.ts`** — New static service class with 11 methods:
   - CRUD: `getAllTasks`, `getTaskById`, `createTask`, `updateTask`, `deleteTask`
   - Inline: `updateField`, `reorderTasks`
   - Queries: `getTasksForUser`, `getTasksForClient`, `getSubtasks`, `getOverdueTasks`, `getTasksDueSoon`
   - Auto-sets `completed_at` on status → complete, clears on un-complete

3. **`app/tasks/page.tsx`** — Refactored all 8 `supabase.from('tasks')` calls to use `TaskService`. Removed inline `Task` type in favor of imported one. Zero UI changes.

Build passes cleanly.

Perfect! Now I have all the information needed. Let me compile the findings:

## Key Findings Summary

### 1. Storage Bucket Migration Pattern
**File**: `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/sql/migrations/018_create_form_attachments_storage.sql`

This migration creates a `form-attachments` bucket with:
- **Public bucket**: `public: true`
- **10MB file size limit**: `file_size_limit: 10485760`
- **Allowed MIME types**: Images (JPEG, PNG, GIF, WebP), PDFs, Word docs, Excel sheets, TXT, CSV
- **Storage policies**: Anyone can upload/read, form owners and admins can delete
- **Path structure**: `formId/fieldId/filename`

### 2. File Upload Component Reference
**File**: `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/components/campaign/FileUploadComponent.tsx`

This is a complete drag-and-drop file upload component you can reference:
- **Drag-drop zone**: `onDragEnter`, `onDragLeave`, `onDragOver`, `onDrop` handlers
- **File input with ref**: `useRef` for programmatic trigger
- **File icon detection**: Based on MIME type (images, videos, PDFs, etc.)
- **Progress tracking**: Displays upload progress for each file with `Progress` component
- **Error handling**: Shows errors per file with toast notifications
- **Metadata storage**: Saves file info to database (name, URL, type, size, uploader)
- **Storage path format**: `campaignId/timestamp-randomId.extension`
- **File removal**: Can cancel uploading files before completion

### 3. Client Service Structure
**File**: `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/lib/clientService.ts`

Client structure for dropdown:
```typescript
type Client = Database['public']['Tables']['clients']['Row'];
// Properties: id, name, email, location, logo_url, archived_at, created_at, etc.

// Methods available:
- getAllClients() // Returns all non-archived clients
- getClientsForUser(userRole, userId) // Role-based filtering
```

### 4. Components/Tasks Directory

```
components/tasks/ (does not exist)

### 5. Dialog/Modal Pattern from Tasks Page
**File**: `/Users/addylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/app/tasks/page.tsx`

Modal pattern used throughout the app:
```typescript
// State management:
const [isFormOpen, setIsFormOpen] = useState(false);
const [editingId, setEditingId] = useState<string | null>(null);
const [form, setForm] = useState({ task_name, assigned_to, due_date, description, status, ... });

// Dialog implementation:
<Dialog open={isFormOpen} onOpenChange={(open) => { 
  if (!open) { 
    setIsFormOpen(false); 
    setEditingId(null); 
  } 
}}>
  <DialogContent className="sm:max-w-[600px] max-h-[85vh] overflow-hidden">
    <DialogHeader>
      <DialogTitle>{editingId ? 'Edit Task' : 'Add Task'}</DialogTitle>
      <DialogDescription>...</DialogDescription>
    </DialogHeader>
    {/* Form content */}
    <DialogFooter>
      <Button onClick={handleSave}>Save</Button>
    </DialogFooter>
  </DialogContent>
</Dialog>
```

### 6. TaskService Current State
**File**: `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/lib/taskService.ts`

Current methods available:
- `getAllTasks()`, `getTaskById()`, `createTask()`, `updateTask()`, `deleteTask()`
- `updateField()` for inline edits
- `reorderTasks()`, `getTasksForUser()`, `getTasksForClient()`, `getSubtasks()`
- `getOverdueTasks()`, `getTasksDueSoon()`

**Missing**: No comment/attachment methods yet - Task #5 needs to add these

### 7. UI Components Used
The project uses Shadcn/UI (Radix UI based):
- Dialog (already in `/components/ui/dialog.tsx`)
- Input, Textarea, Button, Label
- Select, Calendar, Popover
- Tabs, Badge, Tooltip
- Rich Text Editor (React Quill)

All these are available for building the TaskDetailModal and related components.

 `./lib/clientService.ts`

```text
     1→import { supabase } from './supabase';
     2→import { Database } from './database.types';
     3→import { generateUniqueSlug } from './slugUtils';
     4→
     5→type Client = Database['public']['Tables']['clients']['Row'];
     6→type ClientAccessMember = Database['public']['Tables']['client_access_members']['Row'];
     7→
     8→export interface ClientWithAccess extends Client {
     9→  member_count?: number;
    10→  campaign_count?: number;
    11→  whitelist_partner_name?: string;
    12→}
    13→
    14→export class ClientService {
    15→  /**
    16→   * Get clients based on user role
    17→   * - Admins: See all clients
    18→   * - Members: See only clients they have access to via client_access_members
    19→   */
    20→  static async getClientsForUser(userRole: 'super_admin' | 'admin' | 'member' | 'client', userId: string): Promise<ClientWithAccess[]> {
    21→    try {
    22→      if (userRole === 'admin' || userRole === 'super_admin') {
    23→        // Admins and super admins can see all non-archived clients
    24→        const { data: clients, error } = await supabase
    25→          .from('clients')
    26→          .select(`
    27→            *,
    28→            client_access_members(count),
    29→            campaigns!campaigns_client_id_fkey(count),
    30→            whitelist_partner:partners!clients_whitelist_partner_id_fkey(name)
    31→          `)
    32→          .is('archived_at', null)
    33→          .order('created_at', { ascending: false });
    34→
    35→        if (error) throw error;
    36→
    37→        return clients?.map(client => ({
    38→          ...client,
    39→          member_count: client.client_access_members?.[0]?.count || 0,
    40→          campaign_count: (client as any).campaigns?.[0]?.count || 0,
    41→          whitelist_partner_name: (client.whitelist_partner as any)?.name
    42→        })) || [];
    43→      } else {
    44→        // Members can only see non-archived clients they have access to
    45→        const { data: clientAccess, error } = await supabase
    46→          .from('client_access_members')
    47→          .select(`
    48→            clients!inner(
    49→              id,
    50→              name,
    51→              email,
    52→              location,
    53→              is_active,
    54→              archived_at,
    55→              created_at,
    56→              updated_at,
    57→              logo_url,
    58→              campaigns!campaigns_client_id_fkey(count),
    59→              whitelist_partner:partners!clients_whitelist_partner_id_fkey(name)
    60→            )
    61→          `)
    62→          .eq('user_id', userId);
    63→
    64→        if (error) throw error;
    65→
    66→        return clientAccess?.map(access => {
    67→          const client = (access as any).clients;
    68→          return {
    69→            ...client,
    70→            campaign_count: client.campaigns?.[0]?.count || 0,
    71→            whitelist_partner_name: (client.whitelist_partner as any)?.name
    72→          };
    73→        }).filter(c => c && !c.archived_at) || [];
    74→      }
    75→    } catch (error) {
    76→      console.error('Error fetching clients:', error);
    77→      throw error;
    78→    }
    79→  }
    80→
    81→  /**
    82→   * Get all non-archived clients (admin only)
    83→   */
    84→  static async getAllClients(): Promise<Client[]> {
    85→    try {
    86→      const { data, error } = await supabase
    87→        .from('clients')
    88→        .select('*')
    89→        .is('archived_at', null)
    90→        .order('created_at', { ascending: false });
    91→
    92→      if (error) throw error;
    93→      return data || [];
    94→    } catch (error) {
    95→      console.error('Error fetching all clients:', error);
    96→      throw error;
    97→    }
    98→  }
    99→
   100→  /**
   101→   * Create a new client
   102→   */
   103→  static async createClient(
   104→    name: string,
   105→    email: string,
   106→    location?: string,
   107→    source?: string,
   108→    onboarding_call_held?: boolean,
   109→    onboarding_call_date?: string | null,
   110→    is_whitelisted?: boolean,
   111→    whitelist_partner_id?: string | null
   112→  ): Promise<Client> {
   113→    try {
   114→      // Generate a unique slug from the client name
   115→      const slug = generateUniqueSlug(name);
   116→
   117→      const { data, error } = await supabase
   118→        .from('clients')
   119→        .insert({ name, email, location, source, onboarding_call_held, onboarding_call_date, is_whitelisted, whitelist_partner_id, slug })
   120→        .select()
   121→        .single();
   122→      if (error) throw error;
   123→      return data;
   124→    } catch (error) {
   125→      console.error('Error creating client:', error);
   126→      throw error;
   127→    }
   128→  }
   129→
   130→  /**
   131→   * Update client
   132→   */
   133→  static async updateClient(id: string, updates: Partial<Pick<Client, 'name' | 'email' | 'location' | 'is_active' | 'is_whitelisted' | 'whitelist_partner_id' | 'logo_url' | 'approved_domains'>>): Promise<Client> {
   134→    try {
   135→      const { data, error } = await supabase
   136→        .from('clients')
   137→        .update(updates)
   138→        .eq('id', id)
   139→        .select()
   140→        .single();
   141→
   142→      if (error) throw error;
   143→      return data;
   144→    } catch (error) {
   145→      console.error('Error updating client:', error);
   146→      throw error;
   147→    }
   148→  }
   149→
   150→  /**
   151→   * Grant user access to a client
   152→   */
   153→  static async grantClientAccess(clientId: string, userId: string): Promise<ClientAccessMember> {
   154→    try {
   155→      const { data, error } = await supabase
   156→        .from('client_access_members')
   157→        .insert({ client_id: clientId, user_id: userId })
   158→        .select()
   159→        .single();
   160→
   161→      if (error) throw error;
   162→      return data;
   163→    } catch (error) {
   164→      console.error('Error granting client access:', error);
   165→      throw error;
   166→    }
   167→  }
   168→
   169→  /**
   170→   * Revoke user access from a client
   171→   */
   172→  static async revokeClientAccess(clientId: string, userId: string): Promise<void> {
   173→    try {
   174→      const { error } = await supabase
   175→        .from('client_access_members')
   176→        .delete()
   177→        .eq('client_id', clientId)
   178→        .eq('user_id', userId);
   179→
   180→      if (error) throw error;
   181→    } catch (error) {
   182→      console.error('Error revoking client access:', error);
   183→      throw error;
   184→    }
   185→  }
   186→
   187→  /**
   188→   * Get users with access to a specific client
   189→   */
   190→  static async getClientMembers(clientId: string): Promise<any[]> {
   191→    try {
   192→      const { data, error } = await supabase
   193→        .from('client_access_members')
   194→        .select(`
   195→          *,
   196→          users!inner(id, name, email, role)
   197→        `)
   198→        .eq('client_id', clientId);
   199→
   200→      if (error) throw error;
   201→      return data?.map(access => access.users) || [];
   202→    } catch (error) {
   203→      console.error('Error fetching client members:', error);
   204→      throw error;
   205→    }
   206→  }
   207→
   208→  /**
   209→   * Get client by ID or slug (for portal access)
   210→   */
   211→  static async getClientByIdOrSlug(identifier: string): Promise<Client | null> {
   212→    try {
   213→      // Check if identifier is a UUID
   214→      const isUUID = /^[0-9a-f]{8}-[0-9a-f]{4}-[0-9a-f]{4}-[0-9a-f]{4}-[0-9a-f]{12}$/i.test(identifier);
   215→
   216→      const query = supabase
   217→        .from('clients')
   218→        .select('*')
   219→        .is('archived_at', null);
   220→
   221→      if (isUUID) {
   222→        query.eq('id', identifier);
   223→      } else {
   224→        query.eq('slug', identifier);
   225→      }
   226→
   227→      const { data, error } = await query.single();
   228→
   229→      if (error) {
   230→        if (error.code === 'PGRST116') return null; // Not found
   231→        throw error;
   232→      }
   233→      return data;
   234→    } catch (error) {
   235→      console.error('Error fetching client by ID or slug:', error);
   236→      throw error;
   237→    }
   238→  }
   239→
   240→  /**
   241→   * Get client with their campaigns for portal display
   242→   */
   243→  static async getClientWithCampaigns(clientId: string): Promise<{
   244→    client: Client;
   245→    campaigns: any[];
   246→  } | null> {
   247→    try {
   248→      // Get client
   249→      const { data: client, error: clientError } = await supabase
   250→        .from('clients')
   251→        .select('*')
   252→        .eq('id', clientId)
   253→        .is('archived_at', null)
   254→        .single();
   255→
   256→      if (clientError || !client) return null;
   257→
   258→      // Get campaigns for this client with KOL and content counts
   259→      const { data: campaigns, error: campaignsError } = await supabase
   260→        .from('campaigns')
   261→        .select(`
   262→          id,
   263→          name,
   264→          slug,
   265→          status,
   266→          total_budget,
   267→          start_date,
   268→          end_date,
   269→          region,
   270→          description,
   271→          share_report_publicly,
   272→          created_at,
   273→          campaign_kols(count),
   274→          contents(
   275→            impressions,
   276→            likes,
   277→            comments,
   278→            retweets,
   279→            bookmarks
   280→          )
   281→        `)
   282→        .eq('client_id', clientId)
   283→        .is('archived_at', null)
   284→        .order('start_date', { ascending: false });
   285→
   286→      if (campaignsError) throw campaignsError;
   287→
   288→      // Process campaigns to add aggregated metrics
   289→      const processedCampaigns = campaigns?.map(campaign => {
   290→        const contents = (campaign as any).contents || [];
   291→        const totalImpressions = contents.reduce((sum: number, c: any) => sum + (c.impressions || 0), 0);
   292→        const totalEngagement = contents.reduce((sum: number, c: any) =>
   293→          sum + (c.likes || 0) + (c.comments || 0) + (c.retweets || 0) + (c.bookmarks || 0), 0);
   294→
   295→        return {
   296→          ...campaign,
   297→          kol_count: (campaign as any).campaign_kols?.[0]?.count || 0,
   298→          content_count: contents.length,
   299→          total_impressions: totalImpressions,
   300→          total_engagement: totalEngagement,
   301→        };
   302→      }) || [];
   303→
   304→      return { client, campaigns: processedCampaigns };
   305→    } catch (error) {
   306→      console.error('Error fetching client with campaigns:', error);
   307→      throw error;
   308→    }
   309→  }
   310→}
```
</details></tool-use>

 `./sql/migrations/018_create_form_attachments_storage.sql`

```text
     1→-- Create storage bucket for form attachments
     2→-- This allows public form submissions to upload files
     3→
     4→-- Create the storage bucket
     5→INSERT INTO storage.buckets (id, name, public, file_size_limit, allowed_mime_types)
     6→VALUES (
     7→  'form-attachments',
     8→  'form-attachments',
     9→  true, -- Public bucket so anyone can read the files
    10→  10485760, -- 10MB file size limit
    11→  ARRAY[
    12→    'image/jpeg',
    13→    'image/png',
    14→    'image/gif',
    15→    'image/webp',
    16→    'application/pdf',
    17→    'application/msword',
    18→    'application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.wordprocessingml.document',
    19→    'application/vnd.ms-excel',
    20→    'application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet',
    21→    'text/plain',
    22→    'text/csv'
    23→  ]
    24→)
    25→ON CONFLICT (id) DO NOTHING;
    26→
    27→-- Create storage policies
    28→
    29→-- Allow anyone to upload files (for public form submissions)
    30→CREATE POLICY "Anyone can upload form attachments"
    31→  ON storage.objects FOR INSERT
    32→  WITH CHECK (
    33→    bucket_id = 'form-attachments'
    34→  );
    35→
    36→-- Allow anyone to read files (public bucket)
    37→CREATE POLICY "Anyone can read form attachments"
    38→  ON storage.objects FOR SELECT
    39→  USING (
    40→    bucket_id = 'form-attachments'
    41→  );
    42→
    43→-- Allow form owners and admins to delete attachments
    44→CREATE POLICY "Form owners and admins can delete attachments"
    45→  ON storage.objects FOR DELETE
    46→  USING (
    47→    bucket_id = 'form-attachments'
    48→    AND (
    49→      -- Check if user owns the form (path format: formId/fieldId/filename)
    50→      EXISTS (
    51→        SELECT 1 FROM forms
    52→        WHERE forms.id::text = split_part(name, '/', 1)
    53→        AND forms.user_id = auth.uid()
    54→      )
    55→      OR
    56→      -- Check if user is admin
    57→      EXISTS (
    58→        SELECT 1 FROM users
    59→        WHERE users.id = auth.uid()
    60→        AND users.role = 'admin'
    61→      )
    62→    )
    63→  );
    64→
    65→-- Add comment for documentation
    66→COMMENT ON POLICY "Anyone can upload form attachments" ON storage.objects IS
    67→  'Allows anyone (including anonymous users) to upload attachments when submitting forms';
    68→
    69→COMMENT ON POLICY "Anyone can read form attachments" ON storage.objects IS
    70→  'Allows public access to read form attachments since the bucket is public';
    71→
    72→COMMENT ON POLICY "Form owners and admins can delete attachments" ON storage.objects IS
    73→  'Allows form owners to delete attachments for their forms, and admins to delete any attachment';
    74→
```

 `./components/campaign/FileUploadComponent.tsx`

```text
     1→'use client';
     2→
     3→import { useState, useRef } from 'react';
     4→import { Button } from '@/components/ui/button';
     5→import { Progress } from '@/components/ui/progress';
     6→import { Upload, X, File, Image as ImageIcon, FileText, Video } from 'lucide-react';
     7→import { supabase } from '@/lib/supabase';
     8→import { useToast } from '@/hooks/use-toast';
     9→
    10→interface FileUploadComponentProps {
    11→  campaignId: string;
    12→  onUploadSuccess: () => void;
    13→}
    14→
    15→interface UploadingFile {
    16→  file: File;
    17→  progress: number;
    18→  error?: string;
    19→}
    20→
    21→export function FileUploadComponent({ campaignId, onUploadSuccess }: FileUploadComponentProps) {
    22→  const [uploadingFiles, setUploadingFiles] = useState<UploadingFile[]>([]);
    23→  const [isDragActive, setIsDragActive] = useState(false);
    24→  const fileInputRef = useRef<HTMLInputElement>(null);
    25→  const { toast } = useToast();
    26→
    27→  const getFileIcon = (fileType: string) => {
    28→    if (fileType.startsWith('image/')) return <ImageIcon className="h-8 w-8 text-blue-500" />;
    29→    if (fileType.startsWith('video/')) return <Video className="h-8 w-8 text-purple-500" />;
    30→    if (fileType.includes('pdf')) return <FileText className="h-8 w-8 text-red-500" />;
    31→    return <File className="h-8 w-8 text-gray-500" />;
    32→  };
    33→
    34→  const uploadFile = async (file: File) => {
    35→    try {
    36→      // Add file to uploading state
    37→      setUploadingFiles(prev => [...prev, { file, progress: 0 }]);
    38→
    39→      // Create unique file name
    40→      const fileExt = file.name.split('.').pop();
    41→      const fileName = `${campaignId}/${Date.now()}-${Math.random().toString(36).substring(7)}.${fileExt}`;
    42→
    43→      // Upload to Supabase Storage
    44→      const { data: uploadData, error: uploadError } = await supabase.storage
    45→        .from('campaign-report-files')
    46→        .upload(fileName, file, {
    47→          cacheControl: '3600',
    48→          upsert: false,
    49→        });
    50→
    51→      if (uploadError) throw uploadError;
    52→
    53→      // Update progress
    54→      setUploadingFiles(prev =>
    55→        prev.map(f => f.file === file ? { ...f, progress: 50 } : f)
    56→      );
    57→
    58→      // Get public URL
    59→      const { data: { publicUrl } } = supabase.storage
    60→        .from('campaign-report-files')
    61→        .getPublicUrl(fileName);
    62→
    63→      // Save file metadata to database
    64→      const { error: dbError } = await supabase
    65→        .from('campaign_report_files')
    66→        .insert({
    67→          campaign_id: campaignId,
    68→          file_name: file.name,
    69→          file_url: publicUrl,
    70→          file_type: file.type,
    71→          file_size: file.size,
    72→          is_public: false,
    73→          uploaded_by: (await supabase.auth.getUser()).data.user?.id || null,
    74→          display_order: 0,
    75→        });
    76→
    77→      if (dbError) throw dbError;
    78→
    79→      // Update progress to complete
    80→      setUploadingFiles(prev =>
    81→        prev.map(f => f.file === file ? { ...f, progress: 100 } : f)
    82→      );
    83→
    84→      // Remove from uploading after a delay
    85→      setTimeout(() => {
    86→        setUploadingFiles(prev => prev.filter(f => f.file !== file));
    87→      }, 1000);
    88→
    89→      toast({
    90→        title: 'Success',
    91→        description: `${file.name} uploaded successfully`,
    92→      });
    93→
    94→      onUploadSuccess();
    95→    } catch (error: any) {
    96→      console.error('Upload error:', error);
    97→      setUploadingFiles(prev =>
    98→        prev.map(f => f.file === file ? { ...f, error: error.message } : f)
    99→      );
   100→
   101→      toast({
   102→        title: 'Upload failed',
   103→        description: error.message || 'Failed to upload file',
   104→        variant: 'destructive',
   105→      });
   106→    }
   107→  };
   108→
   109→  const handleFiles = async (files: FileList | null) => {
   110→    if (!files) return;
   111→    for (let i = 0; i < files.length; i++) {
   112→      await uploadFile(files[i]);
   113→    }
   114→  };
   115→
   116→  const handleDrag = (e: React.DragEvent) => {
   117→    e.preventDefault();
   118→    e.stopPropagation();
   119→  };
   120→
   121→  const handleDragIn = (e: React.DragEvent) => {
   122→    e.preventDefault();
   123→    e.stopPropagation();
   124→    setIsDragActive(true);
   125→  };
   126→
   127→  const handleDragOut = (e: React.DragEvent) => {
   128→    e.preventDefault();
   129→    e.stopPropagation();
   130→    setIsDragActive(false);
   131→  };
   132→
   133→  const handleDrop = (e: React.DragEvent) => {
   134→    e.preventDefault();
   135→    e.stopPropagation();
   136→    setIsDragActive(false);
   137→    handleFiles(e.dataTransfer.files);
   138→  };
   139→
   140→  const removeUploadingFile = (file: File) => {
   141→    setUploadingFiles(prev => prev.filter(f => f.file !== file));
   142→  };
   143→
   144→  return (
   145→    <div className="space-y-4">
   146→      <div
   147→        onDragEnter={handleDragIn}
   148→        onDragLeave={handleDragOut}
   149→        onDragOver={handleDrag}
   150→        onDrop={handleDrop}
   151→        onClick={() => fileInputRef.current?.click()}
   152→        className={`border-2 border-dashed rounded-lg p-8 text-center cursor-pointer transition-colors ${
   153→          isDragActive
   154→            ? 'border-[#3e8692] bg-[#3e8692]/5'
   155→            : 'border-gray-300 hover:border-[#3e8692] hover:bg-gray-50'
   156→        }`}
   157→      >
   158→        <input
   159→          ref={fileInputRef}
   160→          type="file"
   161→          multiple
   162→          onChange={(e) => handleFiles(e.target.files)}
   163→          className="hidden"
   164→        />
   165→        <Upload className="h-12 w-12 mx-auto mb-4 text-gray-400" />
   166→        {isDragActive ? (
   167→          <p className="text-lg font-medium text-[#3e8692]">Drop files here...</p>
   168→        ) : (
   169→          <>
   170→            <p className="text-lg font-medium text-gray-700 mb-2">
   171→              Drag & drop files here, or click to browse
   172→            </p>
   173→            <p className="text-sm text-gray-500">
   174→              Any file type supported
   175→            </p>
   176→          </>
   177→        )}
   178→      </div>
   179→
   180→      {uploadingFiles.length > 0 && (
   181→        <div className="space-y-3">
   182→          <h4 className="font-semibold text-sm text-gray-700">Uploading...</h4>
   183→          {uploadingFiles.map((uploadingFile, index) => (
   184→            <div
   185→              key={index}
   186→              className="flex items-center gap-3 p-3 border border-gray-200 rounded-lg"
   187→            >
   188→              {getFileIcon(uploadingFile.file.type)}
   189→              <div className="flex-1 min-w-0">
   190→                <p className="text-sm font-medium text-gray-900 truncate">
   191→                  {uploadingFile.file.name}
   192→                </p>
   193→                <p className="text-xs text-gray-500">
   194→                  {(uploadingFile.file.size / 1024 / 1024).toFixed(2)} MB
   195→                </p>
   196→                {uploadingFile.error ? (
   197→                  <p className="text-xs text-red-600 mt-1">{uploadingFile.error}</p>
   198→                ) : (
   199→                  <div className="mt-2">
   200→                    <div className="relative h-2 w-full overflow-hidden rounded-full bg-gray-200">
   201→                      <div
   202→                        className="h-full transition-all duration-300"
   203→                        style={{
   204→                          width: `${uploadingFile.progress}%`,
   205→                          backgroundColor: '#3e8692',
   206→                        }}
   207→                      />
   208→                    </div>
   209→                  </div>
   210→                )}
   211→              </div>
   212→              <Button
   213→                variant="ghost"
   214→                size="sm"
   215→                onClick={() => removeUploadingFile(uploadingFile.file)}
   216→                disabled={uploadingFile.progress === 100}
   217→              >
   218→                <X className="h-4 w-4" />
   219→              </Button>
   220→            </div>
   221→          ))}
   222→        </div>
   223→      )}
   224→    </div>
   225→  );
   226→}
   227→
```

'use client';

import { useState, useEffect, useMemo, useRef, useCallback } from 'react';
import { Button } from '@/components/ui/button';
import { Input } from '@/components/ui/input';
import { Label } from '@/components/ui/label';

import { Calendar } from '@/components/ui/calendar';
import { Popover, PopoverContent, PopoverTrigger } from '@/components/ui/popover';
import { Dialog, DialogContent, DialogDescription, DialogFooter, DialogHeader, DialogTitle } from '@/components/ui/dialog';
import { Skeleton } from '@/components/ui/skeleton';
import { Tabs, TabsList, TabsTrigger } from '@/components/ui/tabs';
import { Badge } from '@/components/ui/badge';

import 'react-quill/dist/quill.snow.css';

const ReactQuill = dynamic(() => import('react-quill'), { ssr: false });

  Plus,
  ListTodo,
  Search,
  Trash2,
  Edit,
  Calendar as CalendarIcon,
  ChevronUp,
  ChevronDown,
  ChevronRight,
  ExternalLink,
  ClipboardList,
  Expand,
  User,
  GripVertical,
  Circle,
  CheckCircle2,
  PauseCircle,
  PlayCircle,
  MessageCircle,
} from 'lucide-react';


type TeamMember = {
  id: string;
  name: string;
  email: string;
  role: string;
  profile_photo_url: string | null;
};

type EditingCell = { taskId: string; field: string } | null;

const FREQUENCIES = ['one-time', 'daily', 'weekly', 'monthly', 'recurring'] as const;
const FREQUENCY_LABELS: Record<string, string> = {
  'one-time': 'One-Time',
  'daily': 'Daily',
  'weekly': 'Weekly',
  'monthly': 'Monthly',
  'recurring': 'Recurring',
};
const TASK_TYPES = [
  'Admin & Operations',
  'Finance & Invoicing',
  'General',
  'Tech & Tools',
  'Marketing & Sales',
  'Client SOP',
  'Client Delivery',
  'Performance Review',
  'Research & Analytics',
] as const;

const STATUSES = ['to_do', 'in_progress', 'paused', 'ready_for_feedback', 'complete'] as const;
const STATUS_CONFIG: Record<string, { label: string; icon: typeof Circle; color: string; bg: string }> = {
  to_do: { label: 'To Do', icon: Circle, color: 'text-gray-400', bg: 'hover:bg-gray-100' },
  in_progress: { label: 'In Progress', icon: PlayCircle, color: 'text-blue-500', bg: 'hover:bg-blue-50' },
  paused: { label: 'Paused', icon: PauseCircle, color: 'text-amber-500', bg: 'hover:bg-amber-50' },
  ready_for_feedback: { label: 'Ready for Feedback', icon: MessageCircle, color: 'text-purple-500', bg: 'hover:bg-purple-50' },
  complete: { label: 'Complete', icon: CheckCircle2, color: 'text-green-500', bg: 'hover:bg-green-50' },
};

const frequencyBadge = (freq: string) => {
  switch (freq) {
    case 'one-time': return 'bg-gray-100 text-gray-700';
    case 'daily': return 'bg-blue-100 text-blue-800';
    case 'weekly': return 'bg-green-100 text-green-800';
    case 'monthly': return 'bg-purple-100 text-purple-800';
    case 'recurring': return 'bg-orange-100 text-orange-800';
    default: return 'bg-gray-100 text-gray-700';
  }
};

const typeBadge = (type: string) => {
  switch (type) {
    case 'Admin & Operations': return 'bg-slate-100 text-slate-700';
    case 'Finance & Invoicing': return 'bg-emerald-100 text-emerald-800';

   100→    case 'Finance & Invoicing': return 'bg-emerald-100 text-emerald-800';

   106→    case 'Performance Review': return 'bg-violet-100 text-violet-800';

   108→    default: return 'bg-gray-100 text-gray-700';
   109→  }
   110→};
   111→
   112→const getDueDateColor = (dueDate: string | null) => {
   113→  if (!dueDate) return '';
   114→  const today = new Date();
   115→  today.setHours(0, 0, 0, 0);
   116→  const due = new Date(dueDate + 'T00:00:00');
   117→  const diffDays = Math.ceil((due.getTime() - today.getTime()) / (1000 * 60 * 60 * 24));
   118→  if (diffDays < 0) return 'text-red-600 font-semibold';
   119→  if (diffDays <= 3) return 'text-amber-600 font-semibold';
   120→  return 'text-gray-600';
   121→};
   122→
   123→/** Format a Date to YYYY-MM-DD in local timezone (avoids UTC offset issues) */
   124→const toLocalDateString = (date: Date) => {
   125→  const year = date.getFullYear();
   126→  const month = String(date.getMonth() + 1).padStart(2, '0');
   127→  const day = String(date.getDate()).padStart(2, '0');
   128→  return `${year}-${month}-${day}`;
   129→};
   130→
   131→// Fixed column widths for consistency across all tables
   132→const COL: Record<string, string> = {
   133→  reorder: 'w-[50px]',
   134→  status: 'w-[36px]',
   135→  taskName: 'w-[22%] min-w-[180px]',
   136→  dueDate: 'w-[110px]',
   137→  comment: 'w-[14%] min-w-[100px]',
   138→  frequency: 'w-[100px]',
   139→  type: 'w-[140px]',
   140→  link: 'w-[80px]',
   141→  createdBy: 'w-[100px]',
   142→  created: 'w-[80px]',
   143→  actions: 'w-[80px]',
   144→};
   145→
   146→type ColumnKey = 'taskName' | 'dueDate' | 'comment' | 'frequency' | 'type' | 'link' | 'createdBy' | 'created';
   147→
   148→const COLUMN_DEFS: { key: ColumnKey; label: string }[] = [
   149→  { key: 'taskName', label: 'Task Name' },
   150→  { key: 'dueDate', label: 'Due Date' },
   151→  { key: 'comment', label: 'Comment' },
   152→  { key: 'frequency', label: 'Frequency' },
   153→  { key: 'type', label: 'Type' },
   154→  { key: 'link', label: 'Link' },
   155→  { key: 'createdBy', label: 'Created By' },
   156→  { key: 'created', label: 'Created' },
   157→];
   158→
   159→const DEFAULT_COLUMN_ORDER: ColumnKey[] = COLUMN_DEFS.map(c => c.key);
   160→const COLUMN_ORDER_KEY = 'tasks-column-order';
   161→
   162→export default function TasksPage() {
   163→  const { user, userProfile } = useAuth();
   164→  const { toast } = useToast();
   165→
   166→  const [tasks, setTasks] = useState<Task[]>([]);
   167→  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(true);
   168→  const [teamMembers, setTeamMembers] = useState<TeamMember[]>([]);
   169→  const [activeTab, setActiveTab] = useState<string>('one-time');
   170→  const [searchTerm, setSearchTerm] = useState('');
   171→  const [collapsedUsers, setCollapsedUsers] = useState<Set<string>>(new Set());
   172→
   173→  // Column order (persisted to localStorage)
   174→  const [columnOrder, setColumnOrder] = useState<ColumnKey[]>(DEFAULT_COLUMN_ORDER);
   175→  const dragColRef = useRef<ColumnKey | null>(null);
   176→  const dragOverColRef = useRef<ColumnKey | null>(null);
   177→
   178→  useEffect(() => {
   179→    try {
   180→      const saved = localStorage.getItem(COLUMN_ORDER_KEY);
   181→      if (saved) {
   182→        const parsed = JSON.parse(saved) as ColumnKey[];
   183→        // Validate: must contain exactly the same keys
   184→        if (parsed.length === DEFAULT_COLUMN_ORDER.length && DEFAULT_COLUMN_ORDER.every(k => parsed.includes(k))) {
   185→          setColumnOrder(parsed);
   186→        }
   187→      }
   188→    } catch {}
   189→  }, []);
   190→
   191→  const handleColumnDragStart = (col: ColumnKey) => {
   192→    dragColRef.current = col;
   193→  };
   194→
   195→  const handleColumnDragOver = (e: React.DragEvent, col: ColumnKey) => {
   196→    e.preventDefault();
   197→    dragOverColRef.current = col;
   198→  };
   199→
   200→  const handleColumnDrop = () => {
   201→    const from = dragColRef.current;
   202→    const to = dragOverColRef.current;
   203→    dragColRef.current = null;
   204→    dragOverColRef.current = null;
   205→    if (!from || !to || from === to) return;
   206→
   207→    setColumnOrder(prev => {
   208→      const newOrder = [...prev];
   209→      const fromIdx = newOrder.indexOf(from);
   210→      const toIdx = newOrder.indexOf(to);
   211→      newOrder.splice(fromIdx, 1);
   212→      newOrder.splice(toIdx, 0, from);
   213→      localStorage.setItem(COLUMN_ORDER_KEY, JSON.stringify(newOrder));
   214→      return newOrder;
   215→    });
   216→  };
   217→
   218→  // Inline editing state
   219→  const [editingCell, setEditingCell] = useState<EditingCell>(null);
   220→  const [editingValue, setEditingValue] = useState<string>('');
   221→
   222→  // Adding new row inline (pipeline pattern)
   223→  const [addingToGroup, setAddingToGroup] = useState<string | null>(null);
   224→  const [newRowName, setNewRowName] = useState('');
   225→
   226→  // Form state
   227→  const [isFormOpen, setIsFormOpen] = useState(false);
   228→  const [editingId, setEditingId] = useState<string | null>(null);
   229→  const [deletingId, setDeletingId] = useState<string | null>(null);
   230→  const [submitting, setSubmitting] = useState(false);
   231→  const [form, setForm] = useState({
   232→    task_name: '',
   233→    assigned_to: '' as string,
   234→    due_date: undefined as Date | undefined,
   235→    frequency: '' as string,
   236→    task_type: '' as string,
   237→    link: '',
   238→    latest_comment: '',
   239→    description: '',
   240→    status: 'to_do' as string,
   241→  });
   242→
   243→  useEffect(() => {
   244→    fetchTasks();
   245→    UserService.getAllUsers().then((users) => {
   246→      setTeamMembers(
   247→        users
   248→          .filter(u => u.role !== 'client')
   249→          .map(u => ({ id: u.id, name: u.name || u.email, email: u.email, role: u.role, profile_photo_url: u.profile_photo_url || null }))
   250→      );
   251→    });
   252→  }, []);
   253→
   254→  const fetchTasks = async () => {
   255→    setLoading(true);
   256→    try {
   257→      const data = await TaskService.getAllTasks();
   258→      setTasks(data);
   259→    } catch (error) {
   260→      console.error('Error fetching tasks:', error);
   261→    }
   262→    setLoading(false);
   263→  };
   264→
   265→  // Build a map of user id -> profile photo
   266→  const userPhotoMap = useMemo(() => {
   267→    const map: Record<string, string | null> = {};
   268→    teamMembers.forEach(m => { map[m.id] = m.profile_photo_url; });
   269→    return map;
   270→  }, [teamMembers]);
   271→
   272→  // Tab counts
   273→  const oneTimeCount = useMemo(() =>
   274→    tasks.filter(t => t.frequency === 'one-time' && t.task_type !== 'Client SOP').length,
   275→    [tasks]
   276→  );
   277→  const recurringCount = useMemo(() =>
   278→    tasks.filter(t => t.frequency !== 'one-time' && t.task_type !== 'Client SOP').length,
   279→    [tasks]
   280→  );
   281→  const clientSopCount = useMemo(() =>
   282→    tasks.filter(t => t.task_type === 'Client SOP').length,
   283→    [tasks]
   284→  );
   285→
   286→  // Filter tasks by tab + search
   287→  const filtered = useMemo(() => {
   288→    let tabFiltered: Task[];
   289→    if (activeTab === 'one-time') {
   290→      tabFiltered = tasks.filter(t => t.frequency === 'one-time' && t.task_type !== 'Client SOP');
   291→    } else if (activeTab === 'recurring') {
   292→      tabFiltered = tasks.filter(t => t.frequency !== 'one-time' && t.task_type !== 'Client SOP');
   293→    } else {
   294→      tabFiltered = tasks.filter(t => t.task_type === 'Client SOP');
   295→    }
   296→
   297→    if (!searchTerm) return tabFiltered.sort((a, b) => a.sort_order - b.sort_order);
   298→
   299→    const term = searchTerm.toLowerCase();
```

```text
     1→import { supabase } from './supabase';
     2→
     3→export type Task = {
     4→  id: string;
     5→  task_name: string;
     6→  assigned_to: string | null;
     7→  assigned_to_name: string | null;
     8→  due_date: string | null;
     9→  latest_comment: string | null;
    10→  frequency: string;
    11→  task_type: string;
    12→  link: string | null;
    13→  description: string | null;
    14→  status: string;
    15→  created_by: string | null;
    16→  created_by_name: string | null;
    17→  sort_order: number;
    18→  created_at: string;
    19→  updated_at: string;
    20→  // New M1 columns
    21→  client_id: string | null;
    22→  parent_task_id: string | null;
    23→  priority: string;
    24→  completed_at: string | null;
    25→  recurring_config: Record<string, any> | null;
    26→  template_id: string | null;
    27→};
    28→
    29→export type TaskInsert = Omit<Task, 'id' | 'created_at' | 'updated_at' | 'sort_order'> & {
    30→  sort_order?: number;
    31→};
    32→
    33→export type TaskUpdate = Partial<Omit<Task, 'id' | 'created_at'>>;
    34→
    35→export class TaskService {
    36→  /**
    37→   * Get all tasks ordered by sort_order then created_at
    38→   */
    39→  static async getAllTasks(): Promise<Task[]> {
    40→    try {
    41→      const { data, error } = await supabase
    42→        .from('tasks')
    43→        .select('*')
    44→        .order('sort_order', { ascending: true })
    45→        .order('created_at', { ascending: false });
    46→
    47→      if (error) throw error;
    48→      return (data as Task[]) || [];
    49→    } catch (error) {
    50→      console.error('Error fetching tasks:', error);
    51→      throw error;
    52→    }
    53→  }
    54→
    55→  /**
    56→   * Get a single task by ID
    57→   */
    58→  static async getTaskById(id: string): Promise<Task | null> {
    59→    try {
    60→      const { data, error } = await supabase
    61→        .from('tasks')
    62→        .select('*')
    63→        .eq('id', id)
    64→        .single();
    65→
    66→      if (error) {
    67→        if (error.code === 'PGRST116') return null;
    68→        throw error;
    69→      }
    70→      return data as Task;
    71→    } catch (error) {
    72→      console.error('Error fetching task:', error);
    73→      throw error;
    74→    }
    75→  }
    76→
    77→  /**
    78→   * Create a new task
    79→   */
    80→  static async createTask(task: Partial<TaskInsert>): Promise<Task> {
    81→    try {
    82→      const { data, error } = await supabase
    83→        .from('tasks')
    84→        .insert(task)
    85→        .select()
    86→        .single();
    87→
    88→      if (error) throw error;
    89→      return data as Task;
    90→    } catch (error) {
    91→      console.error('Error creating task:', error);
    92→      throw error;
    93→    }
    94→  }
    95→
    96→  /**
    97→   * Update a task. Automatically sets completed_at on status change.
    98→   */
    99→  static async updateTask(id: string, updates: TaskUpdate): Promise<Task> {
   100→    try {
   101→      // Handle completed_at logic on status change
   102→      const payload: Record<string, any> = {
   103→        ...updates,
   104→        updated_at: new Date().toISOString(),
   105→      };
   106→
   107→      if (updates.status === 'complete' && !updates.completed_at) {
   108→        payload.completed_at = new Date().toISOString();
   109→      } else if (updates.status && updates.status !== 'complete') {
   110→        payload.completed_at = null;
   111→      }
   112→
   113→      const { data, error } = await supabase
   114→        .from('tasks')
   115→        .update(payload)
   116→        .eq('id', id)
   117→        .select()
   118→        .single();
   119→
   120→      if (error) throw error;
   121→      return data as Task;
   122→    } catch (error) {
   123→      console.error('Error updating task:', error);
   124→      throw error;
   125→    }
   126→  }
   127→
   128→  /**
   129→   * Delete a task
   130→   */
   131→  static async deleteTask(id: string): Promise<void> {
   132→    try {
   133→      const { error } = await supabase
   134→        .from('tasks')
   135→        .delete()
   136→        .eq('id', id);
   137→
   138→      if (error) throw error;
   139→    } catch (error) {
   140→      console.error('Error deleting task:', error);
   141→      throw error;
   142→    }
   143→  }
   144→
   145→  /**
   146→   * Update a single field on a task (for inline edits).
   147→   * Handles completed_at logic for status changes.
   148→   */
   149→  static async updateField(id: string, field: string, value: any): Promise<void> {
   150→    try {
   151→      const payload: Record<string, any> = {
   152→        [field]: value,
   153→        updated_at: new Date().toISOString(),
   154→      };
   155→
   156→      // Handle completed_at on status changes
   157→      if (field === 'status' && value === 'complete') {
   158→        payload.completed_at = new Date().toISOString();
   159→      } else if (field === 'status' && value !== 'complete') {
   160→        payload.completed_at = null;
   161→      }
   162→
   163→      const { error } = await supabase
   164→        .from('tasks')
   165→        .update(payload)
   166→        .eq('id', id);
   167→
   168→      if (error) throw error;
   169→    } catch (error) {
   170→      console.error('Error updating task field:', error);
   171→      throw error;
   172→    }
   173→  }
   174→
   175→  /**
   176→   * Reorder tasks by updating sort_order for multiple tasks
   177→   */
   178→  static async reorderTasks(moves: { id: string; sort_order: number }[]): Promise<void> {
   179→    try {
   180→      await Promise.all(
   181→        moves.map(({ id, sort_order }) =>
   182→          supabase.from('tasks').update({ sort_order }).eq('id', id)
   183→        )
   184→      );
   185→    } catch (error) {
   186→      console.error('Error reordering tasks:', error);
   187→      throw error;
   188→    }
   189→  }
   190→
   191→  /**
   192→   * Get tasks assigned to a specific user
   193→   */
   194→  static async getTasksForUser(userId: string): Promise<Task[]> {
   195→    try {
   196→      const { data, error } = await supabase
   197→        .from('tasks')
   198→        .select('*')
   199→        .eq('assigned_to', userId)
   200→        .order('sort_order', { ascending: true })
   201→        .order('created_at', { ascending: false });
   202→
   203→      if (error) throw error;
   204→      return (data as Task[]) || [];
   205→    } catch (error) {
   206→      console.error('Error fetching tasks for user:', error);
   207→      throw error;
   208→    }
   209→  }
   210→
   211→  /**
   212→   * Get tasks linked to a specific client
   213→   */
   214→  static async getTasksForClient(clientId: string): Promise<Task[]> {
   215→    try {
   216→      const { data, error } = await supabase
   217→        .from('tasks')
   218→        .select('*')
   219→        .eq('client_id', clientId)
   220→        .order('sort_order', { ascending: true })
   221→        .order('created_at', { ascending: false });
   222→
   223→      if (error) throw error;
   224→      return (data as Task[]) || [];
   225→    } catch (error) {
   226→      console.error('Error fetching tasks for client:', error);
   227→      throw error;
   228→    }
   229→  }
   230→
   231→  /**
   232→   * Get subtasks of a parent task
   233→   */
   234→  static async getSubtasks(parentTaskId: string): Promise<Task[]> {
   235→    try {
   236→      const { data, error } = await supabase
   237→        .from('tasks')
   238→        .select('*')
   239→        .eq('parent_task_id', parentTaskId)
   240→        .order('sort_order', { ascending: true });
   241→
   242→      if (error) throw error;
   243→      return (data as Task[]) || [];
   244→    } catch (error) {
   245→      console.error('Error fetching subtasks:', error);
   246→      throw error;
   247→    }
   248→  }
   249→
   250→  /**
   251→   * Get overdue tasks (due_date < today, not complete)
   252→   */
   253→  static async getOverdueTasks(): Promise<Task[]> {
   254→    try {
   255→      const today = new Date().toISOString().split('T')[0];
   256→      const { data, error } = await supabase
   257→        .from('tasks')
   258→        .select('*')
   259→        .lt('due_date', today)
   260→        .neq('status', 'complete')
   261→        .order('due_date', { ascending: true });
   262→
   263→      if (error) throw error;
   264→      return (data as Task[]) || [];
   265→    } catch (error) {
   266→      console.error('Error fetching overdue tasks:', error);
   267→      throw error;
   268→    }
   269→  }
   270→
   271→  /**
   272→   * Get tasks due within the next N days (not complete)
   273→   */
   274→  static async getTasksDueSoon(days: number = 7): Promise<Task[]> {
   275→    try {
   276→      const today = new Date();
   277→      const futureDate = new Date(today);
   278→      futureDate.setDate(futureDate.getDate() + days);
   279→
   280→      const todayStr = today.toISOString().split('T')[0];
   281→      const futureStr = futureDate.toISOString().split('T')[0];
   282→
   283→      const { data, error } = await supabase
   284→        .from('tasks')
   285→        .select('*')
   286→        .gte('due_date', todayStr)
   287→        .lte('due_date', futureStr)
   288→        .neq('status', 'complete')
   289→        .order('due_date', { ascending: true });
   290→
   291→      if (error) throw error;
   292→      return (data as Task[]) || [];
   293→    } catch (error) {
   294→      console.error('Error fetching tasks due soon:', error);
   295→      throw error;
   296→    }
   297→  }
   298→}
   299→
```

Perfect! Now I have all the information needed. Let me compile the findings:

## Key Findings Summary

### 1. Storage Bucket Migration Pattern
**File**: `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/sql/migrations/018_create_form_attachments_storage.sql`

This migration creates a `form-attachments` bucket with:
- **Public bucket**: `public: true`
- **10MB file size limit**: `file_size_limit: 10485760`
- **Allowed MIME types**: Images (JPEG, PNG, GIF, WebP), PDFs, Word docs, Excel sheets, TXT, CSV
- **Storage policies**: Anyone can upload/read, form owners and admins can delete
- **Path structure**: `formId/fieldId/filename`

### 2. File Upload Component Reference
**File**: `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/components/campaign/FileUploadComponent.tsx`

This is a complete drag-and-drop file upload component you can reference:
- **Drag-drop zone**: `onDragEnter`, `onDragLeave`, `onDragOver`, `onDrop` handlers
- **File input with ref**: `useRef` for programmatic trigger
- **File icon detection**: Based on MIME type (images, videos, PDFs, etc.)
- **Progress tracking**: Displays upload progress for each file with `Progress` component
- **Error handling**: Shows errors per file with toast notifications
- **Metadata storage**: Saves file info to database (name, URL, type, size, uploader)
- **Storage path format**: `campaignId/timestamp-randomId.extension`
- **File removal**: Can cancel uploading files before completion

### 3. Client Service Structure
**File**: `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/lib/clientService.ts`

Client structure for dropdown:
```typescript
type Client = Database['public']['Tables']['clients']['Row'];
// Properties: id, name, email, location, logo_url, archived_at, created_at, etc.

// Methods available:
- getAllClients() // Returns all non-archived clients
- getClientsForUser(userRole, userId) // Role-based filtering
```

### 4. Components/Tasks Directory
**Status**: Does NOT exist yet

```
components/tasks/ (does not exist)
```

### 5. Dialog/Modal Pattern from Tasks Page
**File**: `/Users/addylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/app/tasks/page.tsx`

Modal pattern used throughout the app:
```typescript
// State management:
const [isFormOpen, setIsFormOpen] = useState(false);
const [editingId, setEditingId] = useState<string | null>(null);
const [form, setForm] = useState({ task_name, assigned_to, due_date, description, status, ... });

// Dialog implementation:
<Dialog open={isFormOpen} onOpenChange={(open) => { 
  if (!open) { 
    setIsFormOpen(false); 
    setEditingId(null); 
  } 
}}>
  <DialogContent className="sm:max-w-[600px] max-h-[85vh] overflow-hidden">
    <DialogHeader>
      <DialogTitle>{editingId ? 'Edit Task' : 'Add Task'}</DialogTitle>
      <DialogDescription>...</DialogDescription>
    </DialogHeader>
    {/* Form content */}
    <DialogFooter>
      <Button onClick={handleSave}>Save</Button>
    </DialogFooter>
  </DialogContent>
</Dialog>
```

### 6. TaskService Current State
**File**: `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/lib/taskService.ts`

Current methods available:
- `getAllTasks()`, `getTaskById()`, `createTask()`, `updateTask()`, `deleteTask()`
- `updateField()` for inline edits
- `reorderTasks()`, `getTasksForUser()`, `getTasksForClient()`, `getSubtasks()`
- `getOverdueTasks()`, `getTasksDueSoon()`

**Missing**: No comment/attachment methods yet - Task #5 needs to add these

### 7. UI Components Used
The project uses Shadcn/UI (Radix UI based):
- Dialog (already in `/components/ui/dialog.tsx`)
- Input, Textarea, Button, Label
- Select, Calendar, Popover
- Tabs, Badge, Tooltip
- Rich Text Editor (React Quill)

All these are available for building the TaskDetailModal and related components.

---

**Ready for next steps**: The components/tasks directory needs to be created, and migrations for task_comments and task_attachments tables need to be applied before building the detail modal, comments, and attachments components.

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-17 10:30:55Z)**_

<tool-use data-tool-type="read" data-tool-name="Read"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Read**</summary>
 `./sql/migrations/018_create_form_attachments_storage.sql`

```text
     1→-- Create storage bucket for form attachments
     2→-- This allows public form submissions to upload files
     3→
     4→-- Create the storage bucket
     5→INSERT INTO storage.buckets (id, name, public, file_size_limit, allowed_mime_types)
     6→VALUES (
     7→  'form-attachments',
     8→  'form-attachments',
     9→  true, -- Public bucket so anyone can read the files
    10→  10485760, -- 10MB file size limit
    11→  ARRAY[
    12→    'image/jpeg',
    13→    'image/png',
    14→    'image/gif',
    15→    'image/webp',
    16→    'application/pdf',
    17→    'application/msword',
    18→    'application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.wordprocessingml.document',
    19→    'application/vnd.ms-excel',
    20→    'application/vnd.openxmlformats-officedocument.spreadsheetml.sheet',
    21→    'text/plain',
    22→    'text/csv'
    23→  ]
    24→)
    25→ON CONFLICT (id) DO NOTHING;
    26→
    27→-- Create storage policies
    28→
    29→-- Allow anyone to upload files (for public form submissions)
    30→CREATE POLICY "Anyone can upload form attachments"
    31→  ON storage.objects FOR INSERT
    32→  WITH CHECK (
    33→    bucket_id = 'form-attachments'
    34→  );
    35→
    36→-- Allow anyone to read files (public bucket)
    37→CREATE POLICY "Anyone can read form attachments"
    38→  ON storage.objects FOR SELECT
    39→  USING (
    40→    bucket_id = 'form-attachments'
    41→  );
    42→
    43→-- Allow form owners and admins to delete attachments
    44→CREATE POLICY "Form owners and admins can delete attachments"
    45→  ON storage.objects FOR DELETE
    46→  USING (
    47→    bucket_id = 'form-attachments'
    48→    AND (
    49→      -- Check if user owns the form (path format: formId/fieldId/filename)
    50→      EXISTS (
    51→        SELECT 1 FROM forms
    52→        WHERE forms.id::text = split_part(name, '/', 1)
    53→        AND forms.user_id = auth.uid()
    54→      )
    55→      OR
    56→      -- Check if user is admin
    57→      EXISTS (
    58→        SELECT 1 FROM users
    59→        WHERE users.id = auth.uid()
    60→        AND users.role = 'admin'
    61→      )
    62→    )
    63→  );
    64→
    65→-- Add comment for documentation
    66→COMMENT ON POLICY "Anyone can upload form attachments" ON storage.objects IS
    67→  'Allows anyone (including anonymous users) to upload attachments when submitting forms';
    68→
    69→COMMENT ON POLICY "Anyone can read form attachments" ON storage.objects IS
    70→  'Allows public access to read form attachments since the bucket is public';
    71→
    72→COMMENT ON POLICY "Form owners and admins can delete attachments" ON storage.objects IS
    73→  'Allows form owners to delete attachments for their forms, and admins to delete any attachment';
    74→
```
</details></tool-use>
