```text
     1→/**
     2→ * Telegram Bot Service
     3→ * Sends notifications to Telegram chat rooms
     4→ */
     5→
     6→interface TelegramMessage {
     7→  chat_id: string;
     8→  text: string;
     9→  parse_mode?: 'HTML' | 'Markdown' | 'MarkdownV2';
    10→  disable_web_page_preview?: boolean;
    11→  message_thread_id?: number;
    12→}
    13→
    14→export class TelegramService {
    15→  // Read env vars at runtime via getters to avoid serverless caching issues
    16→  private static get botToken() {
    17→    return process.env.TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN;
    18→  }
    19→
    20→  private static get chatId() {
    21→    return process.env.TELEGRAM_TERMINAL_CHAT_ID;
    22→  }
    23→
    24→  private static get threadId() {
    25→    return process.env.TELEGRAM_TERMINAL_THREAD_ID;
    26→  }
    27→
    28→  /**
    29→   * Send a message to the configured Telegram chat
    30→   */
    31→  static async sendMessage(text: string, parseMode: 'HTML' | 'Markdown' = 'HTML'): Promise<boolean> {
    32→    const botToken = this.botToken;
    33→    const chatId = this.chatId;
    34→    const threadId = this.threadId;
    35→
    36→    if (!botToken || !chatId) {
    37→      console.error('[Telegram] Configuration missing:', {
    38→        hasToken: !!botToken,
    39→        hasChatId: !!chatId,
    40→        tokenPrefix: botToken ? botToken.substring(0, 10) + '...' : 'missing',
    41→        chatId: chatId || 'missing'
    42→      });
    43→      return false;
    44→    }
    45→
    46→    try {
    47→      const message: TelegramMessage = {
    48→        chat_id: chatId,
    49→        text,
    50→        parse_mode: parseMode,
    51→        disable_web_page_preview: false,
    52→      };
    53→
    54→      // Add thread ID if configured (for sending to specific topics in groups)
    55→      if (threadId) {
    56→        message.message_thread_id = parseInt(threadId);
    57→      }
    58→
    59→      console.log('[Telegram] Sending message:', {
    60→        chatId: chatId,
    61→        threadId: threadId,
    62→        messageLength: text.length,
    63→        parseMode
    64→      });
    65→
    66→      const response = await fetch(
    67→        `https://api.telegram.org/bot${botToken}/sendMessage`,
    68→        {
    69→          method: 'POST',
    70→          headers: {
    71→            'Content-Type': 'application/json',
    72→          },
    73→          body: JSON.stringify(message),
    74→        }
    75→      );
    76→
    77→      if (!response.ok) {
    78→        const error = await response.json();
    79→        console.error('[Telegram] API error:', {
    80→          status: response.status,
    81→          error
    82→        });
    83→        return false;
    84→      }
    85→
    86→      const result = await response.json();
    87→      console.log('[Telegram] Message sent successfully:', {
    88→        messageId: result.result?.message_id,
    89→        chatId: result.result?.chat?.id
    90→      });
    91→
    92→      return true;
    93→    } catch (error) {
    94→      console.error('[Telegram] Error sending message:', error);
    95→      return false;
    96→    }
    97→  }
    98→
    99→  /**
   100→   * Format and send form submission notification
   101→   */
   102→  static async sendFormSubmissionNotification(
   103→    formName: string,
   104→    formId: string,
   105→    submittedBy?: { name?: string; email?: string },
   106→    responseData?: Record<string, any>
   107→  ): Promise<boolean> {
   108→    // Construct the base URL
   109→    const baseUrl = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_BASE_URL
   110→      ? (process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_BASE_URL.startsWith('http')
   111→          ? process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_BASE_URL
   112→          : `https://${process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_BASE_URL}`)
   113→      : (process.env.VERCEL_URL ? `https://${process.env.VERCEL_URL}` : 'http://localhost:3000');
   114→
   115→    const formUrl = `${baseUrl}/forms/${formId}`;
   116→
   117→    const message = `${formName} Form has been submitted.\n<a href="${formUrl}">View Form</a>`;
   118→
   119→    return this.sendMessage(message);
   120→  }
   121→
   122→  /**
   123→   * Send a test message to verify configuration
   124→   */
   125→  static async sendTestMessage(): Promise<boolean> {
   126→    return this.sendMessage(
   127→      '<b>✅ Telegram Bot Connected!</b>\n\nYour Holo Hive form submission notifications are now active.'
   128→    );
   129→  }
   130→
   131→  // ============================================
   132→  // WEBHOOK MANAGEMENT
   133→  // ============================================
   134→
   135→  /**
   136→   * Register the webhook URL with Telegram
   137→   * Call this once after deploying to set up message tracking
   138→   */
   139→  static async registerWebhook(webhookUrl: string): Promise<{ success: boolean; error?: string }> {
   140→    if (!this.botToken) {
   141→      return { success: false, error: 'TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN not configured' };
   142→    }
   143→
   144→    try {
   145→      const params: any = {
   146→        url: webhookUrl,
   147→        allowed_updates: ['message', 'edited_message'],
   148→        drop_pending_updates: true // Don't process old messages
   149→      };
   150→
   151→      // Add secret token for webhook verification if configured
   152→      const webhookSecret = process.env.TELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_SECRET;
   153→      if (webhookSecret) {
   154→        params.secret_token = webhookSecret;
   155→      }
   156→
   157→      const response = await fetch(
   158→        `https://api.telegram.org/bot${this.botToken}/setWebhook`,
   159→        {
   160→          method: 'POST',
   161→          headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
   162→          body: JSON.stringify(params)
   163→        }
   164→      );
   165→
   166→      const result = await response.json();
   167→
   168→      if (!result.ok) {
   169→        console.error('[Telegram] Webhook registration failed:', result);
   170→        return { success: false, error: result.description };
   171→      }
   172→
   173→      console.log('[Telegram] Webhook registered successfully:', webhookUrl);
   174→      return { success: true };
   175→    } catch (error) {
   176→      console.error('[Telegram] Error registering webhook:', error);
   177→      return { success: false, error: error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'Unknown error' };
   178→    }
   179→  }
   180→
   181→  /**
   182→   * Remove the webhook (stop receiving updates)
   183→   */
   184→  static async deleteWebhook(): Promise<{ success: boolean; error?: string }> {
   185→    if (!this.botToken) {
   186→      return { success: false, error: 'TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN not configured' };
   187→    }
   188→
   189→    try {
   190→      const response = await fetch(
   191→        `https://api.telegram.org/bot${this.botToken}/deleteWebhook`,
   192→        { method: 'POST' }
   193→      );
   194→
   195→      const result = await response.json();
   196→
   197→      if (!result.ok) {
   198→        return { success: false, error: result.description };
   199→      }
   200→
   201→      console.log('[Telegram] Webhook deleted successfully');
   202→      return { success: true };
   203→    } catch (error) {
   204→      console.error('[Telegram] Error deleting webhook:', error);
   205→      return { success: false, error: error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'Unknown error' };
   206→    }
   207→  }
   208→
   209→  /**
   210→   * Get current webhook info
   211→   */
   212→  static async getWebhookInfo(): Promise<any> {
   213→    if (!this.botToken) {
   214→      return { error: 'TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN not configured' };
   215→    }
   216→
   217→    try {
   218→      const response = await fetch(
   219→        `https://api.telegram.org/bot${this.botToken}/getWebhookInfo`
   220→      );
   221→
   222→      return await response.json();
   223→    } catch (error) {
   224→      console.error('[Telegram] Error getting webhook info:', error);
   225→      return { error: error instanceof Error ? error.message : 'Unknown error' };
   226→    }
   227→  }
   228→
   229→  /**
   230→   * Send a message to a specific chat (for CRM integration)
   231→   */
   232→  static async sendToChat(
   233→    chatId: string,
   234→    text: string,
   235→    parseMode: 'HTML' | 'Markdown' = 'HTML'
   236→  ): Promise<boolean> {
   237→    if (!this.botToken) {
   238→      console.error('[Telegram] Bot token not configured');
   239→      return false;
   240→    }
   241→
   242→    try {
   243→      const response = await fetch(
   244→        `https://api.telegram.org/bot${this.botToken}/sendMessage`,
   245→        {
   246→          method: 'POST',
   247→          headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
   248→          body: JSON.stringify({
   249→            chat_id: chatId,
   250→            text,
   251→            parse_mode: parseMode
   252→          })
   253→        }
   254→      );
   255→
   256→      if (!response.ok) {
   257→        const error = await response.json();
   258→        console.error('[Telegram] Send to chat error:', error);
   259→        return false;
   260→      }
   261→
   262→      return true;
   263→    } catch (error) {
   264→      console.error('[Telegram] Error sending to chat:', error);
   265→      return false;
   266→    }
   267→  }
   268→}
   269→
```

```text
     3→// ============================================

    10→  | 'deal_qualified' | 'proposal' | 'negotiation' | 'contract' | 'closed_won' | 'closed_lost'  // Deal stages
    11→  | 'account_active' | 'account_at_risk' | 'account_churned'  // Account stages
    12→  | 'cold_dm' | 'warm' | 'tg_intro' | 'booked' | 'discovery_done' | 'proposal_sent' | 'proposal_call' | 'v2_contract' | 'v2_closed_won' | 'orbit' | 'v2_closed_lost';  // Sales Pipeline v2 stages
    13→
    14→export type AccountType = 'general' | 'channel' | 'campaign' | 'lite' | 'ad_hoc';

    17→export type AffiliateStatus = 'new' | 'active' | 'inactive';

   135→
   136→// ============================================

   139→
   140→export interface CreateAffiliateData {

   143→  category?: string;
   144→  status?: AffiliateStatus;
   145→  commission_model?: string;
   146→  commission_rate?: number;

   154→
   155→export interface CreateOpportunityData {
   156→  name: string;
   157→  stage?: OpportunityStage;
   158→  account_type?: AccountType;
   159→  deal_value?: number;
   160→  currency?: string;
   161→  owner_id?: string;
   162→  source?: OpportunitySource;
   163→  referrer?: string;
   164→  gc?: string;
   165→  affiliate_id?: string;

   171→
   172→export interface CreatePartnerData {
   173→  name: string;
   174→  category?: PartnerCategory;
   175→  focus?: string;
   176→  status?: PartnerStatus;
   177→  owner_id?: string;
   178→  poc_name?: string;

   185→
   186→export interface CreateContactData {
   187→  name: string;
   188→  email?: string;
   189→  telegram_id?: string;
   190→  x_id?: string;
   191→  role?: string;
   192→  category?: string;
   193→  owner_id?: string;
   194→  notes?: string;
   195→}
   196→
   197→// ============================================
   198→// CRM Service Class
   199→// ============================================
   200→
   201→export class CRMService {
   202→  // ----------------------------------------
   203→  // AFFILIATES
   204→  // ----------------------------------------
   205→
   206→  static async getAllAffiliates(): Promise<CRMAffiliate[]> {
   207→    const { data, error } = await supabase
   208→      .from('crm_affiliates')
   209→      .select('*')
   210→      .order('created_at', { ascending: false });
   211→
   212→    if (error) throw error;
   213→    return data || [];
   214→  }
   215→
   216→  static async getAffiliateById(id: string): Promise<CRMAffiliate | null> {
   217→    const { data, error } = await supabase
   218→      .from('crm_affiliates')
   219→      .select('*')
   220→      .eq('id', id)
   221→      .single();
   222→
   223→    if (error) throw error;
   224→    return data;
   225→  }
   226→
   227→  static async createAffiliate(affiliateData: CreateAffiliateData): Promise<CRMAffiliate> {
   228→    const { data, error } = await supabase
   229→      .from('crm_affiliates')
   230→      .insert([affiliateData])
   231→      .select()
   232→      .single();
   233→
   234→    if (error) throw error;
   235→
   236→    // Record stage history
   237→    await this.recordStageHistory('affiliate', data.id, null, affiliateData.status || 'new');
   238→
   239→    return data;
   240→  }
   241→
   242→  static async updateAffiliate(id: string, updates: Partial<CreateAffiliateData>): Promise<CRMAffiliate> {
   243→    // Get current state for stage history
   244→    const current = await this.getAffiliateById(id);
   245→
   246→    const { data, error } = await supabase
   247→      .from('crm_affiliates')
   248→      .update({ ...updates, updated_at: new Date().toISOString() })
   249→      .eq('id', id)
   250→      .select()
   251→      .single();
   252→
   253→    if (error) throw error;
   254→
   255→    // Record stage change if status changed
   256→    if (updates.status && current && current.status !== updates.status) {
   257→      await this.recordStageHistory('affiliate', id, current.status, updates.status);
   258→    }
   259→
   260→    return data;
   261→  }
   262→
   263→  static async deleteAffiliate(id: string): Promise<void> {
   264→    const { error } = await supabase
   265→      .from('crm_affiliates')
   266→      .delete()
   267→      .eq('id', id);
   268→
   269→    if (error) throw error;
   270→  }
   271→
   272→  // ----------------------------------------
   273→  // OPPORTUNITIES
   274→  // ----------------------------------------
   275→
   276→  static async getAllOpportunities(): Promise<CRMOpportunity[]> {
   277→    const { data, error } = await supabase
   278→      .from('crm_opportunities')
   279→      .select(`
   280→        *,
   281→        affiliate:crm_affiliates(*),
   282→        client:clients!crm_opportunities_client_id_fkey(id, name)
   283→      `)
   284→      .order('position', { ascending: true })
   285→      .order('created_at', { ascending: false });
   286→
   287→    if (error) {
   288→      console.error('Error fetching opportunities:', error);
   289→      throw error;
   290→    }
   291→    return data || [];
   292→  }
   293→
   294→  static async getOpportunitiesByStage(stages: OpportunityStage[]): Promise<CRMOpportunity[]> {
   295→    const { data, error } = await supabase
   296→      .from('crm_opportunities')
   297→      .select(`
   298→        *,
   299→        affiliate:crm_affiliates(*),
   300→        client:clients!crm_opportunities_client_id_fkey(id, name)
   301→      `)
   302→      .in('stage', stages)
   303→      .order('created_at', { ascending: false });
   304→
   305→    if (error) throw error;
   306→    return data || [];
   307→  }
   308→
   309→  static async getOpportunityById(id: string): Promise<CRMOpportunity | null> {
   310→    const { data, error } = await supabase
   311→      .from('crm_opportunities')
   312→      .select(`
   313→        *,
   314→        affiliate:crm_affiliates(*),
   315→        client:clients!crm_opportunities_client_id_fkey(id, name)
   316→      `)
   317→      .eq('id', id)
   318→      .single();
   319→
   320→    if (error) throw error;
   321→    return data;
   322→  }
   323→
   324→  static async createOpportunity(opportunityData: CreateOpportunityData): Promise<CRMOpportunity> {
   325→    const { data, error } = await supabase
   326→      .from('crm_opportunities')
   327→      .insert([opportunityData])
   328→      .select()
   329→      .single();
   330→
   331→    if (error) throw error;
   332→
   333→    // Record stage history
   334→    await this.recordStageHistory('opportunity', data.id, null, opportunityData.stage || 'new');
   335→
   336→    return data;
   337→  }
   338→
   339→  static async updateOpportunity(id: string, updates: Partial<CreateOpportunityData>): Promise<CRMOpportunity> {
   340→    // Get current state for stage history
   341→    const current = await this.getOpportunityById(id);
   342→
   343→    const updateData: any = { ...updates, updated_at: new Date().toISOString() };
   344→
   345→    // Set qualified_at when moving to deal stages
   346→    if (updates.stage && ['proposal', 'contract', 'closed_won', 'closed_lost'].includes(updates.stage)) {
   347→      if (current && !current.qualified_at && ['new', 'contacted', 'qualified', 'unqualified', 'nurture'].includes(current.stage)) {
   348→        updateData.qualified_at = new Date().toISOString();
   349→      }
   350→    }
   351→
   352→    // Set closed_at when closing
   353→    if (updates.stage && ['closed_won', 'closed_lost'].includes(updates.stage)) {
   354→      if (current && !current.closed_at) {
   355→        updateData.closed_at = new Date().toISOString();
   356→      }
   357→    }
   358→
   359→    const { data, error } = await supabase
   360→      .from('crm_opportunities')
   361→      .update(updateData)
   362→      .eq('id', id)
   363→      .select()
   364→      .single();
   365→
   366→    if (error) throw error;
   367→
   368→    // Record stage change if stage changed
   369→    if (updates.stage && current && current.stage !== updates.stage) {
   370→      await this.recordStageHistory('opportunity', id, current.stage, updates.stage);
   371→    }
   372→
   373→    return data;
   374→  }
   375→
   376→  static async deleteOpportunity(id: string): Promise<void> {
   377→    const { error } = await supabase
   378→      .from('crm_opportunities')
   379→      .delete()
   380→      .eq('id', id);
   381→
   382→    if (error) throw error;
   383→  }
   384→
   385→  static async updateOpportunityPositions(positions: { id: string; position: number }[]): Promise<void> {
   386→    // Update each opportunity's position
   387→    const updates = positions.map(({ id, position }) =>
   388→      supabase
   389→        .from('crm_opportunities')
   390→        .update({ position, updated_at: new Date().toISOString() })
   391→        .eq('id', id)
   392→    );
   393→
   394→    const results = await Promise.all(updates);
   395→    const errors = results.filter(r => r.error);
   396→    if (errors.length > 0) {
   397→      console.error('Errors updating positions:', errors);
   398→      throw errors[0].error;
   399→    }
   400→  }
   401→
   402→  // ----------------------------------------
   403→  // PARTNERS
   404→  // ----------------------------------------
   405→
   406→  static async getAllPartners(): Promise<CRMPartner[]> {
   407→    const { data, error } = await supabase
   408→      .from('crm_partners')
   409→      .select(`
   410→        *,
   411→        affiliate:crm_affiliates(*)
   412→      `)
   413→      .order('created_at', { ascending: false });
   414→
   415→    if (error) throw error;
   416→    return data || [];
   417→  }
   418→
   419→  static async getPartnerById(id: string): Promise<CRMPartner | null> {
   420→    const { data, error } = await supabase
   421→      .from('crm_partners')
   422→      .select(`
   423→        *,
   424→        affiliate:crm_affiliates(*)
   425→      `)
   426→      .eq('id', id)
   427→      .single();
   428→
   429→    if (error) throw error;
   430→    return data;
   431→  }
   432→
   433→  static async createPartner(partnerData: CreatePartnerData): Promise<CRMPartner> {
   434→    const { data, error } = await supabase
   435→      .from('crm_partners')
   436→      .insert([partnerData])
   437→      .select()
   438→      .single();
   439→
   440→    if (error) throw error;
   441→
   442→    // Record stage history (for status)
   443→    await this.recordStageHistory('partner', data.id, null, partnerData.status || 'active');
   444→
   445→    return data;
   446→  }
   447→
   448→  static async updatePartner(id: string, updates: Partial<CreatePartnerData>): Promise<CRMPartner> {
   449→    // Get current state for stage history
   450→    const current = await this.getPartnerById(id);
   451→
   452→    const { data, error } = await supabase
   453→      .from('crm_partners')
   454→      .update({ ...updates, updated_at: new Date().toISOString() })
   455→      .eq('id', id)
   456→      .select()
   457→      .single();
   458→
   459→    if (error) throw error;
   460→
   461→    // Record status change if changed
   462→    if (updates.status && current && current.status !== updates.status) {
   463→      await this.recordStageHistory('partner', id, current.status, updates.status);
   464→    }
   465→
   466→    return data;
   467→  }
   468→
   469→  static async deletePartner(id: string): Promise<void> {
   470→    const { error } = await supabase
   471→      .from('crm_partners')
   472→      .delete()
   473→      .eq('id', id);
   474→
   475→    if (error) throw error;
   476→  }

   654→  // ----------------------------------------

   657→
   658→  static async recordStageHistory(
   659→    objectType: 'opportunity' | 'partner' | 'affiliate',
   660→    objectId: string,
   661→    fromStage: string | null,
   662→    toStage: string,
   663→    notes?: string
   664→  ): Promise<void> {
   665→    const { data: { user } } = await supabase.auth.getUser();
   666→
   667→    const { error } = await supabase
   668→      .from('crm_stage_history')
   669→      .insert([{
   670→        object_type: objectType,
   671→        object_id: objectId,
   672→        from_stage: fromStage,
   673→        to_stage: toStage,
   674→        changed_by: user?.id || null,
   675→        notes
   676→      }]);
   677→
   678→    if (error) {
   679→      console.error('Error recording stage history:', error);
   680→      // Don't throw - stage history is non-critical
   681→    }
   682→  }
   683→
   684→  static async getStageHistory(objectType: string, objectId: string): Promise<CRMStageHistory[]> {
   685→    const { data, error } = await supabase
   686→      .from('crm_stage_history')
   687→      .select('*')
   688→      .eq('object_type', objectType)
   689→      .eq('object_id', objectId)
   690→      .order('changed_at', { ascending: false });
   691→
   692→    if (error) throw error;
   693→    return data || [];
   694→  }
   695→
   696→  // ----------------------------------------
   697→  // UTILITY METHODS
   698→  // ----------------------------------------
   699→
   700→  static async updateLastContacted(
   701→    type: 'opportunity' | 'partner' | 'affiliate',
   702→    id: string
   703→  ): Promise<void> {
   704→    const tableName = type === 'opportunity' ? 'crm_opportunities' :
   705→                      type === 'partner' ? 'crm_partners' : 'crm_affiliates';
   706→
   707→    const { error } = await supabase
   708→      .from(tableName)
   709→      .update({
   710→        last_contacted_at: new Date().toISOString(),
   711→        updated_at: new Date().toISOString()
   712→      })
   713→      .eq('id', id);
   714→
   715→    if (error) throw error;
   716→  }
   717→
   718→  // ----------------------------------------

   721→
   722→  static getOpportunityStages() {
   723→    return {
   724→      lead: [
   725→        { value: 'new', label: 'New' },
   726→        { value: 'contacted', label: 'Contacted' },
   727→        { value: 'qualified', label: 'Qualified' },
   728→        { value: 'unqualified', label: 'Unqualified' },
   729→        { value: 'nurture', label: 'Nurture' },
   730→        { value: 'dead', label: 'Dead' }
   731→      ],
   732→      deal: [
   733→        { value: 'deal_qualified', label: 'Qualified' },
   734→        { value: 'proposal', label: 'Proposal' },
   735→        { value: 'negotiation', label: 'Negotiation' },
   736→        { value: 'contract', label: 'Contract' },
   737→        { value: 'closed_won', label: 'Closed Won' },
   738→        { value: 'closed_lost', label: 'Closed Lost' }
   739→      ],
   740→      account: [
   741→        { value: 'account_active', label: 'Active' },
   742→        { value: 'account_at_risk', label: 'At Risk' },
   743→        { value: 'account_churned', label: 'Churned' }
   744→      ]
   745→    };
   746→  }
   747→
   748→  static getAccountTypes() {
   749→    return [
   750→      { value: 'general', label: 'General' },
   751→      { value: 'channel', label: 'Channel' },
   752→      { value: 'campaign', label: 'Campaign' },
   753→      { value: 'lite', label: 'Lite' },
   754→      { value: 'ad_hoc', label: 'Ad Hoc' }
   755→    ];
   756→  }
   757→
   758→  static getOpportunitySources() {
   759→    return [
   760→      { value: 'referral', label: 'Referral' },
   761→      { value: 'inbound', label: 'Inbound' },
   762→      { value: 'event', label: 'Event' },
   763→      { value: 'cold_outreach', label: 'Cold Outreach' }
   764→    ];

   766→

   768→    return [
   769→      { value: 'service_provider', label: 'Service Provider' },
   770→      { value: 'investor_vc', label: 'Investor / VC' },
   771→      { value: 'project', label: 'Project' },

   781→    ];

   783→

   788→    ];
   789→  }
   790→
   791→  // ----------------------------------------
   792→  // ANALYTICS HELPERS
   793→  // ----------------------------------------
   794→
   795→  static async getOpportunityMetrics(): Promise<{
   796→    totalLeads: number;
   797→    totalDeals: number;
   798→    totalValue: number;
   799→    wonValue: number;
   800→    conversionRate: number;
   801→  }> {
   802→    const leadStages: OpportunityStage[] = ['new', 'contacted', 'qualified', 'unqualified', 'nurture', 'dead'];
   803→    const dealStages: OpportunityStage[] = ['deal_qualified', 'proposal', 'negotiation', 'contract', 'closed_won', 'closed_lost'];
   804→
   805→    const { data: opportunities, error } = await supabase
   806→      .from('crm_opportunities')
   807→      .select('stage, deal_value');
   808→
   809→    if (error) throw error;
   810→
   811→    const leads = opportunities?.filter(o => leadStages.includes(o.stage as OpportunityStage)) || [];
   812→    const deals = opportunities?.filter(o => dealStages.includes(o.stage as OpportunityStage)) || [];
   813→    const closedWon = opportunities?.filter(o => o.stage === 'closed_won') || [];
   814→    const closed = opportunities?.filter(o => o.stage === 'closed_won' || o.stage === 'closed_lost') || [];
   815→
   816→    const totalValue = deals.reduce((sum, d) => sum + (d.deal_value || 0), 0);
   817→    const wonValue = closedWon.reduce((sum, d) => sum + (d.deal_value || 0), 0);
   818→    const conversionRate = closed.length > 0 ? (closedWon.length / closed.length) * 100 : 0;
   819→
   820→    return {
   821→      totalLeads: leads.length,
   822→      totalDeals: deals.length,
   823→      totalValue,
   824→      wonValue,
   825→      conversionRate
   826→    };
   827→  }
   828→
   829→  // ----------------------------------------
   830→  // TELEGRAM INTEGRATION
   831→  // ----------------------------------------
   832→
   833→  /**
   834→   * Send a message to an opportunity's Telegram group chat
   835→   * and update the last_reply_at timestamp
   836→   */
   837→  static async sendTelegramMessage(
   838→    opportunityId: string,
   839→    message: string
   840→  ): Promise<{ success: boolean; error?: string }> {
   841→    // Get the opportunity to find the gc (chat ID)
   842→    const opportunity = await this.getOpportunityById(opportunityId);
   843→    if (!opportunity) {
   844→      return { success: false, error: 'Opportunity not found' };
   845→    }
   846→
   847→    if (!opportunity.gc) {
   848→      return { success: false, error: 'No Telegram group chat ID configured for this opportunity' };
   849→    }
   850→
   851→    // Send message via Telegram API
   852→    const botToken = process.env.TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN;
   853→    if (!botToken) {
   854→      return { success: false, error: 'Telegram bot not configured' };
   855→    }
   856→
   857→    try {
   858→      const response = await fetch(
   859→        `https://api.telegram.org/bot${botToken}/sendMessage`,
   860→        {
   861→          method: 'POST',
   862→          headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' },
   863→          body: JSON.stringify({
   864→            chat_id: opportunity.gc,
   865→            text: message,
   866→            parse_mode: 'HTML'
   867→          })
   868→        }
   869→      );
   870→
   871→      if (!response.ok) {
   872→        const error = await response.json();
   873→        console.error('[CRM] Telegram send error:', error);
   874→        return { success: false, error: error.description || 'Failed to send message' };
   875→      }
   876→
   877→      // Update last_reply_at
   878→      await supabase
   879→        .from('crm_opportunities')
   880→        .update({
   881→          last_reply_at: new Date().toISOString(),
   882→          updated_at: new Date().toISOString()
   883→        })
   884→        .eq('id', opportunityId);
   885→
   886→      return { success: true };
   887→    } catch (error) {
   888→      console.error('[CRM] Error sending Telegram message:', error);
   889→      return { success: false, error: 'Failed to send message' };
   890→    }
   891→  }
   892→
   893→  /**
   894→   * Get opportunities that haven't been contacted recently
   895→   */
   896→  static async getStaleOpportunities(daysThreshold: number = 7): Promise<CRMOpportunity[]> {
   897→    const thresholdDate = new Date();
   898→    thresholdDate.setDate(thresholdDate.getDate() - daysThreshold);
   899→
   900→    const { data, error } = await supabase
   901→      .from('crm_opportunities')
   902→      .select(`
   903→        *,
   904→        affiliate:crm_affiliates(*)
   905→      `)
   906→      .not('gc', 'is', null)
   907→      .or(`last_message_at.is.null,last_message_at.lt.${thresholdDate.toISOString()}`)
   908→      .not('stage', 'in', '(closed_won,closed_lost,dead,account_churned)')
   909→      .order('last_message_at', { ascending: true, nullsFirst: true });
   910→
   911→    if (error) throw error;
   912→    return data || [];
   913→  }
   914→}
   915→
```

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-haiku-4-5-20251001 2026-03-17 14:11:19Z)**_

Now let me compile all the information I found. Let me create a comprehensive summary:

## Summary: Telegram Service Analysis

I've thoroughly explored the Telegram service in your KOL Campaign Manager. Here's what I found:

### 1. **How Messages Are Sent (API Method & Bot Token)**

**File:** `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/lib/telegramService.ts`

- **API Method:** Uses Telegram's HTTP API via `https://api.telegram.org/bot{botToken}/sendMessage`
- **HTTP Method:** POST with JSON body
- **Bot Token Environment Variable:** `TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN`

The service reads the token at runtime via a static getter (lines 15-18) to avoid serverless caching issues:
```typescript
private static get botToken() {
  return process.env.TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN;
}
```

### 2. **User Chat ID Resolution / Mapping**

**Key Finding:** There is **NO user-to-chat-ID mapping system** currently implemented. Instead:

- **For Opportunities/Group Chats:** The `crm_opportunities` table has a `gc` field (lines 60 in crmService.ts) that stores the Telegram group chat ID directly. This is a direct mapping at the opportunity level, not the user level.
  
- **For Direct Messages:** The `crm_contacts` table has a `telegram_id` field (line 101 in crmService.ts) which stores individual Telegram user IDs.

- **Method:** `TelegramService.sendToChat(chatId, text, parseMode)` is provided (lines 232-267 in telegramService.ts) for sending to any specific chat by ID.

- **CRM Integration:** In crmService.ts (lines 837-891), there's a `sendTelegramMessage()` method that:
  - Fetches the opportunity by ID
  - Extracts the `gc` (group chat ID) from the opportunity record
  - Sends the message to that group chat
  - Updates `last_reply_at` timestamp

### 3. **Message Formatting Helpers**

The service provides two main formatting methods:

**a) Form Submission Notifications** (lines 102-120):
```typescript
static async sendFormSubmissionNotification(
  formName: string,
  formId: string,
  submittedBy?: { name?: string; email?: string },
  responseData?: Record<string, any>
)
```
- Creates HTML formatted message with form name and clickable link to the form
- Parse mode defaults to HTML

**b) Generic Message Sending** (lines 31-97):
```typescript
static async sendMessage(text: string, parseMode: 'HTML' | 'Markdown' = 'HTML')
```
- Default parse mode is HTML
- Supports `disable_web_page_preview` option
- Supports `message_thread_id` for sending to specific topics in group chats

### 4. **Existing Environment Variables for Group/Ops Chat**

**File:** `/Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/.env.local`

Three environment variables are configured:

```
TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN=7996189688:AAF73zNcxoStIGaNxzYdV_75XYd0LhQ6fZw
TELEGRAM_TERMINAL_CHAT_ID=-1002681460515
TELEGRAM_TERMINAL_THREAD_ID=2
```

- **`TELEGRAM_TERMINAL_CHAT_ID`**: The default/ops group chat ID (`-1002681460515`)
- **`TELEGRAM_TERMINAL_THREAD_ID`**: The topic/thread ID within that group (value: `2`) - used for organizing messages in topics

The service reads these via static getters (lines 20-26):
```typescript
private static get chatId() {
  return process.env.TELEGRAM_TERMINAL_CHAT_ID;
}

private static get threadId() {
  return process.env.TELEGRAM_TERMINAL_THREAD_ID;
}
```

### 5. **Additional Features**

The service also includes:
- **Webhook Management** for receiving Telegram updates (lines 135-227)
- **Test Messages** to verify bot connectivity (lines 125-129)
- **Webhook Secret** support via `TELEGRAM_WEBHOOK_SECRET` env var for security

### Current Usage Pattern

The service is integrated into multiple API routes:
- `/app/api/forms/submit/route.ts` - Sends notifications when forms are submitted
- `/app/api/leads/submit/route.ts` - Leads notifications
- `/app/api/links/submit/route.ts` - Links notifications
- `/app/api/partners/submit/route.ts` - Partners notifications
- `/app/api/telegram/webhook/manage/route.ts` - Webhook management

```typescript
import { NextResponse } from 'next/server';
import { createClient } from '@supabase/supabase-js';
import { Database } from '@/lib/database.types';
import { TelegramService } from '@/lib/telegramService';

export const dynamic = 'force-dynamic';

/**
 * Daily 9 AM cron: sends overdue task notifications to each user via TG.
 * Also notifies assignees of stale tasks (no update in 7+ days).
 *
 * Secured by CRON_SECRET header check.
 */
export async function GET(request: Request) {
  const authHeader = request.headers.get('authorization');
  const cronSecret = process.env.CRON_SECRET;

  if (cronSecret && authHeader !== `Bearer ${cronSecret}`) {
    return NextResponse.json({ error: 'Unauthorized' }, { status: 401 });
  }

  const supabaseUrl = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL;
  const supabaseServiceKey = process.env.SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY;

  if (!supabaseUrl || !supabaseServiceKey) {
    return NextResponse.json({ error: 'Server configuration error' }, { status: 500 });
  }

  const supabase = createClient<Database>(supabaseUrl, supabaseServiceKey, {
    auth: { autoRefreshToken: false, persistSession: false },
  });

  try {
    const today = new Date().toISOString().split('T')[0];

    // 1. Get all overdue tasks (due_date < today, not complete/paused)
    const { data: overdueTasks } = await supabase
      .from('tasks')
      .select('id, task_name, assigned_to, assigned_to_name, due_date')
      .lt('due_date', today)
      .not('status', 'in', '("complete","paused")');

    // 2. Get stale tasks (updated_at > 7 days ago, not complete/paused)
    const sevenDaysAgo = new Date();
    sevenDaysAgo.setDate(sevenDaysAgo.getDate() - 7);

    const { data: staleTasks } = await supabase
      .from('tasks')
      .select('id, task_name, assigned_to, assigned_to_name, updated_at')
      .lt('updated_at', sevenDaysAgo.toISOString())
      .not('status', 'in', '("complete","paused")');

    // 3. Get user telegram IDs
    const userIds = new Set<string>();
    for (const t of overdueTasks || []) {
      if (t.assigned_to) userIds.add(t.assigned_to);
    }
    for (const t of staleTasks || []) {
      if (t.assigned_to) userIds.add(t.assigned_to);
    }

    const { data: profiles } = await supabase
      .from('profiles')
      .select('id, name, telegram_id')
      .in('id', Array.from(userIds));

    const tgMap = new Map<string, string>();
    for (const p of profiles || []) {
      if (p.telegram_id) tgMap.set(p.id, p.telegram_id);
    }

    // 4. Group overdue tasks by user
    const overdueByUser = new Map<string, typeof overdueTasks>();
    for (const task of overdueTasks || []) {
      if (!task.assigned_to) continue;
      const existing = overdueByUser.get(task.assigned_to) || [];
      existing.push(task);
      overdueByUser.set(task.assigned_to, existing);
    }

    // 5. Group stale tasks by user
    const staleByUser = new Map<string, typeof staleTasks>();
    for (const task of staleTasks || []) {
      if (!task.assigned_to) continue;
      const existing = staleByUser.get(task.assigned_to) || [];
      existing.push(task);
      staleByUser.set(task.assigned_to, existing);
    }

    let notificationsSent = 0;

    // 6. Send notifications per user
    for (const userId of userIds) {
      const chatId = tgMap.get(userId);
      if (!chatId) continue;

      const userOverdue = overdueByUser.get(userId) || [];
      const userStale = staleByUser.get(userId) || [];

      if (userOverdue.length === 0 && userStale.length === 0) continue;

      let message = '';

      if (userOverdue.length > 0) {
        message += `⚠️ <b>Overdue Tasks (${userOverdue.length})</b>\n`;
        for (const t of userOverdue) {
          const daysOverdue = Math.ceil(
            (new Date().getTime() - new Date(t.due_date + 'T00:00:00').getTime()) / (1000 * 60 * 60 * 24)
          );
          message += `• ${t.task_name} (${daysOverdue}d overdue)\n`;
        }
        message += '\n';
      }

      if (userStale.length > 0) {
        message += `🕰 <b>Stale Tasks (${userStale.length})</b>\n`;
        for (const t of userStale) {
          const daysSinceUpdate = Math.ceil(
            (Date.now() - new Date(t.updated_at).getTime()) / (1000 * 60 * 60 * 24)
          );
          message += `• ${t.task_name} (no update in ${daysSinceUpdate}d)\n`;
        }
      }

      try {
        await TelegramService.sendToChat(chatId, message, 'HTML');
        notificationsSent++;
      } catch (err) {
        console.error(`Failed to send TG alert to user ${userId}:`, err);
      }
    }

    // 7. Also send summary to ops chat
    const totalOverdue = overdueTasks?.length || 0;
    const totalStale = staleTasks?.length || 0;

    if (totalOverdue > 0 || totalStale > 0) {
      const summary = [
        `📋 <b>Daily Task Alert Summary</b>`,
        ``,
        `⚠️ Overdue tasks: <b>${totalOverdue}</b>`,
        `🕰 Stale tasks: <b>${totalStale}</b>`,
        `📨 Notifications sent: <b>${notificationsSent}</b>`,
      ].join('\n');

      try {
        await TelegramService.sendMessage(summary, 'HTML');
      } catch (err) {
        console.error('Failed to send ops summary:', err);
      }
    }

    return NextResponse.json({
      success: true,
      timestamp: new Date().toISOString(),
      overdueTasks: totalOverdue,
      staleTasks: totalStale,
      notificationsSent,
    });
  } catch (error: any) {
    console.error('Alert cron error:', error);
    return NextResponse.json({ error: error.message }, { status: 500 });
  }
}

```

```text
File created successfully at: /Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/app/api/tasks/alerts/route.ts
```
</details></tool-use>

<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```typescript
import { NextResponse } from 'next/server';
import { createClient } from '@supabase/supabase-js';
import { Database } from '@/lib/database.types';
import { TelegramService } from '@/lib/telegramService';

export const dynamic = 'force-dynamic';

/**
 * Weekly cron (Friday 5 PM): sends a summary report of the week's task activity
 * to the ops chat via Telegram.
 *
 * Secured by CRON_SECRET header check.
 */
export async function GET(request: Request) {
  const authHeader = request.headers.get('authorization');
  const cronSecret = process.env.CRON_SECRET;

  if (cronSecret && authHeader !== `Bearer ${cronSecret}`) {
    return NextResponse.json({ error: 'Unauthorized' }, { status: 401 });
  }

  const supabaseUrl = process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL;
  const supabaseServiceKey = process.env.SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY;

  if (!supabaseUrl || !supabaseServiceKey) {
    return NextResponse.json({ error: 'Server configuration error' }, { status: 500 });
  }

  const supabase = createClient<Database>(supabaseUrl, supabaseServiceKey, {
    auth: { autoRefreshToken: false, persistSession: false },
  });

  try {
    const now = new Date();
    const weekAgo = new Date();
    weekAgo.setDate(weekAgo.getDate() - 7);
    const today = now.toISOString().split('T')[0];

    // 1. Tasks completed this week
    const { data: completedThisWeek } = await supabase
      .from('tasks')
      .select('id, task_name, assigned_to_name, completed_at')
      .eq('status', 'complete')
      .gte('completed_at', weekAgo.toISOString());

    // 2. Tasks created this week
    const { count: createdCount } = await supabase
      .from('tasks')
      .select('*', { count: 'exact', head: true })
      .gte('created_at', weekAgo.toISOString());

    // 3. Currently overdue
    const { data: overdueTasks } = await supabase
      .from('tasks')
      .select('id, task_name, assigned_to_name, due_date')
      .lt('due_date', today)
      .not('status', 'in', '("complete","paused")');

    // 4. Stale tasks (no update in 7+ days)
    const { count: staleCount } = await supabase
      .from('tasks')
      .select('*', { count: 'exact', head: true })
      .lt('updated_at', weekAgo.toISOString())
      .not('status', 'in', '("complete","paused")');

    // 5. Tasks in progress
    const { count: inProgressCount } = await supabase
      .from('tasks')
      .select('*', { count: 'exact', head: true })
      .eq('status', 'in_progress');

    // 6. Per-member completion stats
    const memberStats = new Map<string, number>();
    for (const t of completedThisWeek || []) {
      const name = t.assigned_to_name || 'Unassigned';
      memberStats.set(name, (memberStats.get(name) || 0) + 1);
    }

    // Build report message
    const completedCount = completedThisWeek?.length || 0;
    const overdueCount = overdueTasks?.length || 0;

    const lines = [
      `📊 <b>Weekly Task Report</b>`,
      `<i>${weekAgo.toLocaleDateString('en-US', { month: 'short', day: 'numeric' })} – ${now.toLocaleDateString('en-US', { month: 'short', day: 'numeric', year: 'numeric' })}</i>`,
      ``,
      `✅ Completed: <b>${completedCount}</b>`,
      `📝 Created: <b>${createdCount || 0}</b>`,
      `🔄 In Progress: <b>${inProgressCount || 0}</b>`,
      `⚠️ Overdue: <b>${overdueCount}</b>`,
      `🕰 Stale (7d+): <b>${staleCount || 0}</b>`,
    ];

    if (memberStats.size > 0) {
      lines.push(``, `<b>Completed by Member:</b>`);
      const sorted = [...memberStats.entries()].sort((a, b) => b[1] - a[1]);
      for (const [name, count] of sorted) {
        lines.push(`  • ${name}: ${count}`);
      }
    }

    if (overdueCount > 0 && overdueTasks) {
      lines.push(``, `<b>Overdue Tasks:</b>`);
      for (const t of overdueTasks.slice(0, 10)) {
        const daysOverdue = Math.ceil(
          (now.getTime() - new Date(t.due_date + 'T00:00:00').getTime()) / (1000 * 60 * 60 * 24)
        );
        lines.push(`  • ${t.task_name} (${t.assigned_to_name || 'Unassigned'}, ${daysOverdue}d)`);
      }
      if (overdueCount > 10) {
        lines.push(`  ... and ${overdueCount - 10} more`);
      }
    }

    const message = lines.join('\n');

    try {
      await TelegramService.sendMessage(message, 'HTML');
    } catch (err) {
      console.error('Failed to send weekly report:', err);
    }

    return NextResponse.json({
      success: true,
      timestamp: now.toISOString(),
      completed: completedCount,
      created: createdCount || 0,
      overdue: overdueCount,
      stale: staleCount || 0,
      inProgress: inProgressCount || 0,
    });
  } catch (error: any) {
    console.error('Weekly report error:', error);
    return NextResponse.json({ error: error.message }, { status: 500 });
  }
}

```


<tool-use data-tool-type="write" data-tool-name="Write"><details>
<summary>Tool use: **Write**</summary>

```tsx
'use client';

import { useState, useEffect } from 'react';
import { Button } from '@/components/ui/button';
import { Badge } from '@/components/ui/badge';
import { Input } from '@/components/ui/input';
import { Label } from '@/components/ui/label';
import { Select, SelectContent, SelectItem, SelectTrigger, SelectValue } from '@/components/ui/select';
import { Textarea } from '@/components/ui/textarea';
import { Dialog, DialogContent, DialogDescription, DialogFooter, DialogHeader, DialogTitle } from '@/components/ui/dialog';
import { Skeleton } from '@/components/ui/skeleton';
import { TaskService, TaskTemplate } from '@/lib/taskService';
import { useAuth } from '@/contexts/AuthContext';
import { useToast } from '@/hooks/use-toast';
import {
  FileText,
  Plus,
  Trash2,
  Copy,
  ArrowLeft,
  AlertTriangle,
} from 'lucide-react';
import Link from 'next/link';

const TASK_TYPES = [
  'Admin & Operations', 'Finance & Invoicing', 'General', 'Tech & Tools',
  'Marketing & Sales', 'Client SOP', 'Client Delivery', 'Performance Review', 'Research & Analytics',
];
const FREQUENCIES = ['one-time', 'daily', 'weekly', 'monthly', 'recurring'];
const PRIORITIES = ['low', 'medium', 'high', 'urgent'];

export default function TemplatesPage() {
  const { user, userProfile } = useAuth();
  const { toast } = useToast();
  const [loading, setLoading] = useState(true);
  const [templates, setTemplates] = useState<TaskTemplate[]>([]);
  const [showCreateDialog, setShowCreateDialog] = useState(false);
  const [creating, setCreating] = useState(false);

  const [form, setForm] = useState({
    name: '',
    description: '',
    task_name_template: '',
    task_type: 'General',
    frequency: 'one-time',
    priority: 'medium',
  });

  const isAdmin = userProfile?.role === 'admin' || userProfile?.role === 'super_admin';

  useEffect(() => {
    loadTemplates();
  }, []);

  const loadTemplates = async () => {
    try {
      const data = await TaskService.getTemplates();
      setTemplates(data);
    } catch (err) {
      console.error('Error loading templates:', err);
    } finally {
      setLoading(false);
    }
  };

  const handleCreate = async () => {
    if (!form.name || !form.task_name_template) return;
    setCreating(true);
    try {
      await TaskService.createTemplate({
        name: form.name,
        description: form.description || null,
        task_name_template: form.task_name_template,
        task_type: form.task_type,
        frequency: form.frequency,
        priority: form.priority,
        default_assigned_to: null,
        default_client_id: null,
        recurring_config: null,
        checklist_items: [],
        created_by: user?.id || null,
      });
      toast({ title: 'Created', description: 'Template created successfully.' });
      setShowCreateDialog(false);
      setForm({ name: '', description: '', task_name_template: '', task_type: 'General', frequency: 'one-time', priority: 'medium' });
      loadTemplates();
    } catch (err) {
      console.error('Error creating template:', err);
      toast({ title: 'Error', description: 'Failed to create template.', variant: 'destructive' });
    } finally {
      setCreating(false);
    }
  };

  const handleCreateFromTemplate = async (templateId: string) => {
    if (!user?.id || !userProfile) return;
    try {
      const task = await TaskService.createTaskFromTemplate(templateId, {
        created_by: user.id,
        created_by_name: userProfile.name || userProfile.email || 'Unknown',
      });
      if (task) {
        toast({ title: 'Task Created', description: `Created "${task.task_name}" from template.` });
      }
    } catch (err) {
      console.error('Error creating from template:', err);
      toast({ title: 'Error', description: 'Failed to create task from template.', variant: 'destructive' });
    }
  };

  const handleDelete = async (id: string) => {
    try {
      await TaskService.deleteTemplate(id);
      setTemplates(prev => prev.filter(t => t.id !== id));
      toast({ title: 'Deleted', description: 'Template deleted.' });
    } catch (err) {
      console.error('Error deleting template:', err);
    }
  };

  if (!isAdmin) {
    return (
      <div className="min-h-[calc(100vh-64px)] bg-gray-50 flex items-center justify-center">
        <div className="text-center">
          <AlertTriangle className="h-12 w-12 text-amber-400 mx-auto mb-3" />
          <p className="text-gray-600">Admin access required.</p>
        </div>
      </div>
    );
  }

  if (loading) {
    return (
      <div className="min-h-[calc(100vh-64px)] bg-gray-50 p-6 space-y-4">
        <Skeleton className="h-10 w-64" />
        <div className="grid gap-4">
          {[1, 2, 3].map(i => <Skeleton key={i} className="h-24 rounded-lg" />)}
        </div>
      </div>
    );
  }

  return (
    <div className="min-h-[calc(100vh-64px)] bg-gray-50">
      <div className="w-full space-y-4">
        {/* Header */}
        <div className="w-full bg-white border border-gray-200 shadow-sm p-6">
          <div className="flex items-center justify-between">
            <div className="flex items-center gap-3">
              <Link href="/tasks">
                <Button variant="ghost" size="sm" className="h-8 w-8 p-0">
                  <ArrowLeft className="h-4 w-4" />
                </Button>
              </Link>
              <div className="bg-violet-50 p-2 rounded-lg">
                <FileText className="h-6 w-6 text-violet-600" />
              </div>
              <div>
                <h2 className="text-2xl font-bold text-gray-900">Task Templates</h2>
                <p className="text-sm text-gray-500">Reusable task presets for quick creation</p>
              </div>
            </div>
            <Button
              onClick={() => setShowCreateDialog(true)}
              style={{ backgroundColor: '#3e8692', color: 'white' }}
              className="hover:opacity-90"
            >
              <Plus className="h-4 w-4 mr-2" /> New Template
            </Button>
          </div>
        </div>

        {/* Templates List */}
        <div className="bg-white border border-gray-200 shadow-sm rounded-lg">
          <div className="px-4 py-3 border-b border-gray-100 flex items-center gap-2">
            <FileText className="h-4 w-4 text-violet-600" />
            <h3 className="font-semibold text-sm text-gray-900">Templates</h3>
            <Badge variant="secondary" className="text-xs">{templates.length}</Badge>
          </div>

          {templates.length === 0 ? (
            <div className="p-8 text-center">
              <FileText className="h-10 w-10 text-gray-300 mx-auto mb-2" />
              <p className="text-gray-500 text-sm">No templates yet. Create one or save a task as a template.</p>
            </div>
          ) : (
            <div className="divide-y divide-gray-50">
              {templates.map((tmpl) => (
                <div key={tmpl.id} className="px-4 py-3 flex items-center gap-3">
                  <div className="flex-1 min-w-0">
                    <p className="text-sm font-medium text-gray-900">{tmpl.name}</p>
                    <p className="text-xs text-gray-500">
                      {tmpl.task_name_template} &middot; {tmpl.task_type} &middot; {tmpl.priority}
                    </p>
                    {tmpl.description && (
                      <p className="text-xs text-gray-400 mt-0.5 truncate">{tmpl.description.replace(/<[^>]+>/g, '')}</p>
                    )}
                  </div>
                  {tmpl.checklist_items?.length > 0 && (
                    <Badge variant="outline" className="text-xs">{tmpl.checklist_items.length} items</Badge>
                  )}
                  <Button
                    variant="outline"
                    size="sm"
                    className="text-xs"
                    onClick={() => handleCreateFromTemplate(tmpl.id)}
                  >
                    <Copy className="h-3 w-3 mr-1" /> Use
                  </Button>
                  <Button
                    variant="ghost"
                    size="sm"
                    className="h-7 w-7 p-0 text-gray-400 hover:text-red-500"
                    onClick={() => handleDelete(tmpl.id)}
                  >
                    <Trash2 className="h-3.5 w-3.5" />
                  </Button>
                </div>
              ))}
            </div>
          )}
        </div>
      </div>

      {/* Create Template Dialog */}
      <Dialog open={showCreateDialog} onOpenChange={setShowCreateDialog}>
        <DialogContent className="sm:max-w-[500px]">
          <DialogHeader>
            <DialogTitle>Create Template</DialogTitle>
            <DialogDescription>Create a reusable task template. Use {'{{field_name}}'} placeholders in the task name for dynamic values.</DialogDescription>
          </DialogHeader>

          <div className="space-y-4">
            <div className="grid gap-2">
              <Label>Template Name</Label>
              <Input
                value={form.name}
                onChange={(e) => setForm({ ...form, name: e.target.value })}
                placeholder="e.g., Monthly Client Report"
              />
            </div>

            <div className="grid gap-2">
              <Label>Task Name Template</Label>
              <Input
                value={form.task_name_template}
                onChange={(e) => setForm({ ...form, task_name_template: e.target.value })}
                placeholder="e.g., Prepare {{client_name}} monthly report"
              />
            </div>

            <div className="grid gap-2">
              <Label>Description</Label>
              <Textarea
                value={form.description}
                onChange={(e) => setForm({ ...form, description: e.target.value })}
                placeholder="Optional description..."
                rows={3}
              />
            </div>

            <div className="grid grid-cols-3 gap-3">
              <div className="grid gap-2">
                <Label className="text-xs">Task Type</Label>
                <Select value={form.task_type} onValueChange={(v) => setForm({ ...form, task_type: v })}>
                  <SelectTrigger className="text-xs"><SelectValue /></SelectTrigger>
                  <SelectContent>
                    {TASK_TYPES.map(t => <SelectItem key={t} value={t}>{t}</SelectItem>)}
                  </SelectContent>
                </Select>
              </div>

              <div className="grid gap-2">
                <Label className="text-xs">Frequency</Label>
                <Select value={form.frequency} onValueChange={(v) => setForm({ ...form, frequency: v })}>
                  <SelectTrigger className="text-xs"><SelectValue /></SelectTrigger>
                  <SelectContent>
                    {FREQUENCIES.map(f => <SelectItem key={f} value={f}>{f}</SelectItem>)}
                  </SelectContent>
                </Select>
              </div>

              <div className="grid gap-2">
                <Label className="text-xs">Priority</Label>
                <Select value={form.priority} onValueChange={(v) => setForm({ ...form, priority: v })}>
                  <SelectTrigger className="text-xs"><SelectValue /></SelectTrigger>
                  <SelectContent>
                    {PRIORITIES.map(p => <SelectItem key={p} value={p}>{p.charAt(0).toUpperCase() + p.slice(1)}</SelectItem>)}
                  </SelectContent>
                </Select>
              </div>
            </div>
          </div>

          <DialogFooter>
            <Button variant="outline" onClick={() => setShowCreateDialog(false)}>Cancel</Button>
            <Button
              onClick={handleCreate}
              disabled={!form.name || !form.task_name_template || creating}
              style={{ backgroundColor: '#3e8692', color: 'white' }}
              className="hover:opacity-90"
            >
              {creating ? <div className="animate-spin rounded-full h-4 w-4 border-b-2 border-white" /> : 'Create Template'}
            </Button>
          </DialogFooter>
        </DialogContent>
      </Dialog>
    </div>
  );
}

```

```text
File created successfully at: /Users/andylee/Downloads/KOL Campaign Manager/app/tasks/templates/page.tsx
```

All 6 milestones are now complete. Here's a summary of what was implemented:

**M4: Recurring Engine + Automations** (completed)
- `recurring_config` field integrated into TaskDetailModal (form state, payload, RecurringConfigEditor UI)
- Automations page at `/tasks/automations` with rule CRUD and execution log
- Automations link added to sidebar (admin-only)

**M5: TG Bot Alerts + Weekly Reports** (completed)
- `app/api/tasks/alerts/route.ts` — Daily cron: sends per-user overdue + stale task notifications via Telegram, plus ops summary
- `app/api/tasks/weekly-report/route.ts` — Weekly cron: completed/created/overdue/stale summary with per-member breakdown
- Stale badge on task rows (amber "Stale" indicator for tasks not updated in 7+ days)

**M6: Templates + Form→Task** (completed)
- Migration: `task_templates` and `form_task_mappings` tables with RLS
- Template CRUD + `saveTaskAsTemplate` + `createTaskFromTemplate` in TaskService
- Form→Task mapping CRUD in TaskService
- Templates page at `/tasks/templates` with create/use/delete
- "Save as Template" button in TaskDetailModal footer
- Templates link added to sidebar (admin-only)

All builds pass, no new security advisories.

_**User (2026-03-17 14:19:39Z)**_

Can you give me each milestone summary and how to test them

---

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-17 14:20:24Z)**_



Here's the full summary of all 6 milestones and how to test each:

---

## M1: Foundation (Service Layer + Schema)

**What was done:**
- Added columns to `tasks` table: `client_id`, `parent_task_id`, `priority`, `completed_at`, `recurring_config`, `template_id`
- Added indexes on `assigned_to`, `client_id`, `status`, `due_date`, `parent_task_id`
- Created `lib/taskService.ts` — static service class with all CRUD methods, inline edit helpers, query methods
- Refactored `app/tasks/page.tsx` to use `TaskService` instead of direct Supabase calls

**How to test:**
- Go to `/tasks` — all existing task CRUD should work identically (create, edit, delete, reorder, inline edit)
- Open browser console — no errors from the service layer
- Verify new columns exist in Supabase dashboard under the `tasks` table

---

## M2: Comments, Attachments, Client Linking

**What was done:**
- Created `task_comments` table (threaded via `parent_comment_id`) and `task_attachments` table with RLS
- Created private `task-attachments` storage bucket (10MB limit)
- Added comment/attachment CRUD to TaskService
- Created `TaskComments.tsx`, `TaskAttachments.tsx`, `TaskDetailModal.tsx`
- Added "Client" column to the tasks table

**How to test:**
- Go to `/tasks` → click a task row to open the detail modal
- **Comments tab:** Add a comment → it appears. Click reply → threaded reply appears indented. Edit/delete your own comments
- **Attachments tab:** Drag-and-drop a file or click to upload → file appears in list. Click download → file downloads. Delete works
- **Client column:** In the main table, use the client dropdown on any task row → client name appears. Open task detail → Client dropdown works
- **Comment count:** The comment column in the table should show a count badge

---

## M3: Dashboards + Subtasks/Checklists

**What was done:**
- Created `task_checklist_items` table with RLS
- Added checklist CRUD and dashboard query methods to TaskService
- Created `TaskChecklist.tsx` and `SubtaskList.tsx` components
- Added Checklist and Subtasks tabs to TaskDetailModal
- Created three new pages: `/tasks/my-dashboard`, `/tasks/admin`, `/tasks/client/[clientId]`
- Added sub-navigation in sidebar under Tasks

**How to test:**
- **Checklist:** Open a task → Checklist tab → add items, toggle them done/undone, edit text, delete. Progress bar updates
- **Subtasks:** Open a task → Subtasks tab → add a subtask with a name. It appears as a child task. Toggle its status
- **My Dashboard** (`/tasks/my-dashboard`): Shows your assigned tasks grouped by overdue, due this week, in progress, recently completed. Stat cards at top
- **Admin Overview** (`/tasks/admin`): Only visible if you're admin/super_admin. Shows overall stats, per-member task counts, per-client list. Click a client → goes to client task view
- **Client View** (`/tasks/client/[clientId]`): Shows tasks linked to that client with active/completed split
- **Sidebar:** When on any `/tasks/*` page, sub-nav appears: All Tasks, My Dashboard, Admin Overview (admin only)

---

## M4: Recurring Engine + Automations

**What was done:**
- Created `task_automations` and `task_automation_logs` tables with RLS
- Added recurring auto-clone logic: when a task with `recurring_config` is marked complete, a new instance is auto-created with the next due date
- `calculateNextDueDate()` supports daily/weekly/monthly with day-of-week and day-of-month options
- Created `app/api/tasks/cron/route.ts` — daily safety net for missed recurring instances
- Created `RecurringConfig.tsx` editor component, integrated into TaskDetailModal
- Created `/tasks/automations` page with rule CRUD and execution log
- Added Automations link to sidebar (admin only)

**How to test:**
- **Recurring config:** Open a task → Details tab → scroll to "Recurring" section → click "Enable recurring" → set frequency to Weekly, pick a day of week → save
- **Auto-clone on complete:** Set a recurring task's status to "Complete" → refresh `/tasks` → a new instance should appear with the next due date and status "To Do"
- **Cron endpoint:** Hit `GET /api/tasks/cron` (no auth needed if `CRON_SECRET` is not set) → returns JSON with `recurringGenerated` and `staleTaskCount`
- **Automations page** (`/tasks/automations`): Click "New Rule" → set name, trigger (e.g., Status Change), action (e.g., Send Telegram Notification), scope → Create. Toggle active/inactive. Delete. Execution log shows recurring clone entries

---

## M5: TG Bot Alerts + Weekly Reports

**What was done:**
- Created `app/api/tasks/alerts/route.ts` — daily 9 AM cron: sends per-user Telegram notifications for overdue and stale tasks, plus an ops chat summary
- Created `app/api/tasks/weekly-report/route.ts` — Friday 5 PM cron: sends completed/created/overdue/stale summary with per-member breakdown to ops chat
- Added amber "Stale" badge on task rows in `/tasks` for tasks not updated in 7+ days (excluding complete/paused)

**How to test:**
- **Stale badge:** Find or create a task that hasn't been updated in 7+ days → it should show an amber "Stale" badge next to the task name on the tasks table
- **Daily alerts endpoint:** Hit `GET /api/tasks/alerts` → returns JSON with `overdueTasks`, `staleTasks`, `notificationsSent`. If users have `telegram_id` set in their profile and have overdue/stale tasks, they'll receive a TG message
- **Weekly report endpoint:** Hit `GET /api/tasks/weekly-report` → returns JSON with stats. The ops Telegram chat (configured via `TELEGRAM_TERMINAL_CHAT_ID`) receives the formatted report
- **Prerequisites for TG:** Ensure `TELEGRAM_BOT_TOKEN` and `TELEGRAM_TERMINAL_CHAT_ID` env vars are set. Users need `telegram_id` in the `users` table for per-user alerts

---

## M6: Templates + Form→Task

**What was done:**
- Created `task_templates` and `form_task_mappings` tables with RLS
- Added template CRUD, `saveTaskAsTemplate()`, `createTaskFromTemplate()` (with `{{placeholder}}` substitution), and form→task mapping CRUD to TaskService
- Created `/tasks/templates` page with create/use/delete
- Added "Save as Template" button in TaskDetailModal footer (visible when editing)
- Added Templates link to sidebar (admin only)

**How to test:**
- **Create template from scratch:** Go to `/tasks/templates` → click "New Template" → fill in name, task name template (e.g., `Prepare {{client_name}} report`), type, frequency, priority → Create
- **Use template:** On the templates page, click "Use" on any template → a new task is created with the template's values. Check `/tasks` to see it
- **Save task as template:** Open any existing task → in the dialog footer, click "Save as Template" → toast confirms. Go to `/tasks/templates` → the new template appears with the task's name, type, priority, and checklist items
- **Sidebar:** Templates link appears under Tasks sub-nav for admin/super_admin users

---

**Cron scheduling note:** The `/api/tasks/cron`, `/api/tasks/alerts`, and `/api/tasks/weekly-report` endpoints are ready but need a cron scheduler (e.g., Vercel Cron in `vercel.json` or an external service) to call them on schedule. You can test them manually by visiting the URLs directly.

---
